## Full happy-path flow to implement

### Bước 0 — Navigate
- URL_BASE = `https://wwws-usa2.givex.com/cws4.0/lushusa/`
- Cookie banner: `#button--accept-cookies`
- Buy eGift link: `#cardForeground a[href*='Buy-E-gift-Cards']`

### Bước 1 — eGift Page (`https://wwws-usa2.givex.com/cws4.0/lushusa/e-gifts/`)
Fields to fill:
- **To (Recipient Email)**: `input#giftCardEmail` or `input[name="giftCardEmail"]`
- **Recipient Name**: `input#recipientName` or `input[name="recipientName"]`
- **Message/Greeting**: `textarea#giftMessage` or `textarea[name="giftMessage"]`
- **From (Sender Name)**: `input#senderName` or `input[name="senderName"]`
- **Amount**: `input.denomination-input` or `input[name="amount"]` (type the amount value)

Actions:
- Click **ADD TO CART**: `button.add-to-cart-button` or `button[data-action="add-to-cart"]`
- Wait for **REVIEW & CHECKOUT** button to appear: `a.review-checkout-button` or `a[href*="cart"]`
- Click **REVIEW & CHECKOUT**

### Bước 2 — Cart & Guest Checkout
- At cart page: click **BEGIN CHECKOUT**: `button.begin-checkout` or `a[href*="checkout"]`
- At checkout page: click **CHECKOUT AS GUEST**: `#guestHeading` (this is a clickable heading/button)
- **Guest Email** field: `#guestEmail` or `input[name="guestEmail"]`
- Click **CONTINUE**: `button#guestContinue` or `button[data-action="guest-continue"]`

### Bước 3 — Shipping / Billing Form
Fields:
- First Name: `input#firstName` or `input[name="firstName"]`
- Last Name: `input#lastName` or `input[name="lastName"]`
- Address: `input#address1` or `input[name="address1"]`
- City: `input#city` or `input[name="city"]`
- State: `select#state` or `select[name="state"]`
- ZIP: `input#zipCode` or `input[name="zipCode"]`
- Phone: `input#phone` or `input[name="phone"]`
- Email: `input#email` or `input[name="email"]` (in billing section)
- Click **CONTINUE** to proceed to payment: `button.billing-continue` or `button[data-action="billing-continue"]`

### Bước 4 — Payment / Card
Fields:
- Card Number: `input#creditCardNumber` or `input[name="creditCardNumber"]`
- Expiry Month: `select#expirationMonth` or `select[name="expirationMonth"]`
- Expiry Year: `select#expirationYear` or `select[name="expirationYear"]`
- CVV: `input#securityCode` or `input[name="securityCode"]`
- Submit: `button.complete-purchase` or `button[data-action="complete-purchase"]` (text: "COMPLETE PURCHASE")

### Bước 5 — Post-submit States
- **Success**: URL contains `/order-confirmation` OR `/confirmation` OR element `.order-confirmation` present
- **VBV/3DS iframe**: `iframe[src*="3dsecure"]` OR `iframe[src*="adyen"]` OR `iframe[id*="threeds"]`
- **VBV Cancel button** (inside iframe): `button[id*="cancel"]` OR `a[id*="cancel"]` OR `button:contains("Cancel")` OR `a:contains("Return to")`
- **Declined**: `.payment-error` OR `.error-message` OR `div[data-error]` OR element containing text "declined" OR "transaction failed"
- **UI Lock / spinner**: `.loading-overlay` OR `.spinner` OR `div[aria-busy="true"]`
- **Error popup close**: `button.modal-close` OR `button[aria-label="Close"]` OR `.modal button[type="button"]`
- **Neutral div** (for ui_lock focus-shift): `body`

Replace the selector section at the top of `driver.py` (lines 20-62) with these REAL selectors:

title: Setup repo structure, .gitignore, and professional README
body:
Bootstraps the repository to meet required structural and workflow conventions: root directories, a comprehensive `.gitignore`, and a professional English `README.md`.

## Directory structure
- Added `/phase1`, `/phase2`, `/phase3`, `/src`, `/scripts`, `/docs` as required root directories
- Each directory includes a `README.md` describing its scope and contents

## `.gitignore`
Covers all common exclusion categories:
- Runtime artifacts: `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `build/`, `.cache/`
- Secrets & config: `.env`, `*.env`, `*.pem`, `*.key`, `secrets/`
- User data: `uploads/`, `logs/`, `*.log`
- IaC state: `.terraform/`, `*.tfstate`, `*.tfvars`
- OS/IDE noise: `.DS_Store`, `.vscode/`, `.idea/`
- Language build outputs: Python `__pycache__`, Java `target/`, etc.

## README.md
Rewrites the placeholder `# DEVOPS` with a full professional document:
- Repo structure table mapping each directory to its purpose
- **Branch strategy** section: feature-branch workflow, no direct commits to `main`, all changes via PR with ≥1 reviewer approval and clear description
- Contributing guide using [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) format
- Team table and getting-started instructions

> **Note:** Branch protection rules on `main` (require PR, require review) must be enabled by a repo admin in **Settings → Branches → Branch protection rules** — this cannot be enforced via committed files.

title: Rewrite LogOS as lightweight symbolic Python service with web UI
body:
### Motivation
- Modernize the old Java prototype into a small, resource-efficient symbolic core with a browser-based interface to prioritize symbolic/hypergraph reasoning and use deep learning only where necessary.

### Description
- Add a lightweight `logos` package implementing a minimal hypergraph (`logos/hypergraph.py`), symbolic fact memory (`logos/memory.py`), intent extractor (`logos/nlp.py`), conversation state (`logos/state.py`), rule-based reasoner (`logos/reasoner.py`), and an orchestrating chat engine (`logos/engine.py`).
- Add a FastAPI server at `app/main.py` and a static browser UI in `web/index.html` and `web/styles.css` that talk to `/api/chat` for interactive demo usage.
- Add `requirements.txt` with runtime dependencies and update `README.md` with quick start instructions and a module map describing the rewrite and where legacy Java code remains.

### Testing
- Attempted `pip install -r requirements.txt`, but the install failed due to network/proxy restrictions so dependencies could not be installed.
- Attempted to run the server via `uvicorn app.main:app`, but `uvicorn` was not available in the environment and the server could not be started.
- No automated tests were executed because dependency installation and server start were blocked by the environment, so only static code and README changes were added.

------

title: Update CaixeiroViajante.java
body:
Esse código resolve um problema tipo caixeiro viajante usando a estratégia do “vizinho mais próximo”: a cada passo, ele escolhe o ponto não visitado mais perto, soma a distância ao custo total e segue até visitar todos, depois volta ao início.

A segunda parte só mede desempenho: roda o algoritmo várias vezes com matrizes aleatórias, calcula o tempo de execução e mostra a média em milissegundos.

title: Main.java
body:
apresenta um menu para o usuário escolher entre executar testes automáticos ou fazer uma busca manual, e no caso dos testes, trabalha com conjuntos de dados de tamanhos variados, gerando valores aleatórios para simular um cenário real de uso.

Quando os experimentos são executados, o programa aplica três tipos de busca: sequencial, binária e em árvore, várias vezes sobre os mesmos dados, medindo o tempo de execução de cada uma. No final, ele calcula e exibe a média e o desvio padrão desses tempos, permitindo analisar qual algoritmo é mais eficiente em cada situação.

title: MergeSort.java
body:
Esse código implementa o algoritmo Merge Sort para ordenar um vetor de números inteiros em ordem crescente. Ele funciona dividindo o vetor recursivamente em partes menores até chegar a subvetores de tamanho 1, e depois vai juntando (mesclando) essas partes de forma ordenada.

A função `dividir` separa o vetor em duas metades repetidamente, e a função `mesclar` é responsável por comparar os elementos dessas metades e reorganizá-los no vetor original já em ordem. No final, o vetor inteiro fica ordenado de forma eficiente, com complexidade de tempo O(n log n).

title: docs: Supabase to Convex Migration Guide & Platform Comparison
body:
## Summary

Add comprehensive documentation for evaluating and potentially migrating from Supabase to Convex. This PR includes detailed technical analysis, migration instructions, and recommendations.

**Status**: Documentation only - **migration NOT recommended** at this time.

---

## 📄 Documents Added

### 1. `docs/SUPABASE_TO_CONVEX_MIGRATION.md`
Complete step-by-step migration guide covering:
- **6-Phase Migration Plan** (8 weeks estimated)
- **Schema Conversion** (PostgreSQL SQL → TypeScript)
- **Backend Rewrite** (Python/FastAPI → TypeScript/Convex)
- **Frontend Refactoring** (REST/WebSocket → Convex hooks)
- **Data Migration** (Export/Import scripts)
- **Testing & Deployment**
- **Rollback Plan**
- **Cost Analysis**

### 2. `docs/DATABASE_COMPARISON.md`
Comprehensive platform comparison with 12 sections:
- Database Architecture (SQL vs Document)
- Real-time Capabilities
- Query Performance Benchmarks
- Schema Management
- Backend Integration
- Type Safety
- Cost Comparison ($25 vs $200-400/month)
- Developer Experience
- SilverWall-Specific Impact
- Recommendation Matrix
- Final Verdict

---

## 🔍 Key Findings

### Migration Effort
- **Time**: 6-8 weeks full-time development
- **Risk**: HIGH (complete backend rewrite)
- **Impact**: Must rewrite all 3000+ lines of Python backend to TypeScript

### What Would Be Lost
❌ All 9 existing optimization PRs (#1-#9):
  - Rate limiting (slowapi)
  - Circuit breaker (pybreaker)
  - Connection pooling (httpx)
  - Query caching
  - Database indexes
  - Observability middleware
  - Error handling
  - WebSocket backoff
  - Environment config

### Cost Impact
- **Current (Supabase)**: $25/month Pro tier
- **Projected (Convex)**: $200-400/month for high-traffic
  - Reason: Function call-based pricing
  - 43M requests/month × $3.5/M = expensive

### Team Impact
- Python expertise would be unused
- Team would need TypeScript retraining
- No benefit to existing optimizations

---

## ✅ Recommendation: **STAY WITH SUPABASE**

### Reasons to Stay

1. ✅ **Already Optimized** - 9 PRs of production-ready improvements
2. ✅ **Python Expertise** - Team knows FastAPI, no rewrite needed
3. ✅ **Cost-Effective** - 8-16x cheaper for our traffic
4. ✅ **SQL Power** - Complex queries for standings/analytics
5. ✅ **Low Risk** - No 6-8 week refactor required
6. ✅ **Near Production** - Ready to deploy now

### Alternative: Enhance Supabase Instead

If real-time improvements are needed:
- **Option A**: Add Supabase Realtime (PostgreSQL pub/sub)
- **Option B**: Add Redis for hot-path caching (telemetry)
- **Cost**: $30/month total (vs. $200+ Convex)
- **Time**: 1-2 weeks (vs. 6-8 weeks migration)
- **Risk**: Low (vs. High)

---

## 🎯 Use Cases for This Documentation

### When to Reference This Guide

1. **v2.0 Rewrite** - After shipping and validating current version
2. **Team Shift** - If moving to full TypeScript stack
3. **New Microservice** - Isolate one feature as proof-of-concept
4. **Next Project** - Greenfield F1 project with clean slate

### Convex Is Great For

- ✅ New TypeScript projects
- ✅ Real-time-heavy apps (chat, collaboration)
- ✅ Simple schema with few relationships
- ✅ Rapid prototyping

### Supabase Is Better For

- ✅ Existing Python/Go/Java backends (our case)
- ✅ Complex SQL queries (JOINs, aggregations)
- ✅ High request volume (cost-sensitive)
- ✅ Fine-grained performance control

---

## 📊 Documentation Structure

### SUPABASE_TO_CONVEX_MIGRATION.md
- Table of Contents
- Architecture Overview
- Prerequisites (checklist)
- 6-Phase Implementation Plan
- Code Examples (Before/After)
- Rollback Plan
- Cost Analysis
- Final Recommendation

### DATABASE_COMPARISON.md
- Executive Summary (comparison table)
- 12 Detailed Comparisons
- Benchmark Results
- SilverWall-Specific Analysis
- Recommendation Matrix
- Decision Guidelines

---

## 🚦 Next Steps

### Immediate
- [x] Merge this documentation PR
- [ ] Keep Supabase architecture
- [ ] Focus on deploying existing PRs #1-#9

### Future (If Needed)
- [ ] Evaluate Supabase Realtime for live standings
- [ ] Consider Redis for high-frequency telemetry caching
- [ ] Revisit Convex for v2.0 or next project

---

## 📚 Related PRs

This documentation references:
- PR #3: Backend Rate Limiting
- PR #4: Frontend Response Validation
- PR #5: Backend Performance & Stability
- PR #6: Frontend WebSocket Backoff
- PR #7: Backend Data Processing
- PR #8: Centralized API Client
- PR #9: Backend Observability
- PR #10: Database Query Optimization
- PR #11: Frontend Environment Config

All of these PRs would need to be reimplemented in TypeScript if migrating to Convex.

---

## ✅ Checklist

- [x] Created comprehensive migration guide
- [x] Added technical comparison
- [x] Included code examples (before/after)
- [x] Documented cost analysis
- [x] Provided clear recommendation
- [x] Outlined alternative approaches
- [x] Added rollback plan

---

**Verdict**: This documentation serves as a reference for future architectural decisions. Current recommendation is to **stay with Supabase** and focus on production deployment.

Updates `org.jobrunr:jobrunr` from 8.4.1 to 8.4.2
Release notes

Sourced from org.jobrunr:jobrunr's releases.

v8.4.2

JobRunr and JobRunr Pro 8.4.2

We're shipping 8.4.2 with a fix that matters if you're running Quarkus: the recurring job recorder now works correctly with Quarkus 3.31.1+. The release also includes improved database migration error logging, and documentation updates for ThreadLocalJobContext.getJobContext() as an alternative to the parametric JobContext approach.

For JobRunr Pro Enterprise users, we've made improvements to the MultiClusterDashboard and EmbeddedDashboards.

JobRunr 8.4.2

Enhancements

  • Log statement with table prefix in case of an exception in runMigrationStatement. [PR #1481](jobrunr/jobrunr#1481)
  • Document ThreadLocalJobContext.getJobContext(). [PR #1482](jobrunr/jobrunr#1482)

Bugfixes

  • Fix JobRunrRecurringJobRecorder. [PR #1478](jobrunr/jobrunr#1478)

Misc

  • Fix AssertJ vulnerability. [PR #1476](jobrunr/jobrunr#1476)

  • [MultiClusterDashboard] Always set a JobMapper to the StorageProvider. [PR #792](jobrunr/jobrunr-pro#792)

Bugfixes

  • Fix Spring Boot static files CSP header settings. [PR #786](jobrunr/jobrunr-pro#786)

Updates `ch.qos.logback:logback-classic` from 1.5.27 to 1.5.28
Release notes

Sourced from ch.qos.logback:logback-classic's releases.

Logback 1.5.28

2026-02-06 Release of logback version 1.5.28

• Appender names or appender references are no longer subject to variable substitution.

• Fixed issue with configurations with conditionals encompassing appenders. This was reported in issues/1016 reported by Sergey Sazonov.

• The element now admits a 'scan' attribute which can be used to override the 'scan' attribute in the element.

• Fixed NullPointerException thrown by VersionUtil.checkForVersionEquality method occurring with GraalVM Native Images. This issue was reported in issues/1014.

• A bit-wise identical binary of this version can be reproduced by building from source code at commit e7a1855ab562bb102333f754603ff89359bf3cfc associated with the tag v_1.5.28. Release built using Java "21" 2023-10-17 LTS build 21.0.1.+12-LTS-29 under Linux Debian 11.6.

Updates `org.springframework:spring-framework-bom` from 6.2.15 to 6.2.16
Release notes

Sourced from org.springframework:spring-framework-bom's releases.

v6.2.16

:star: New Features

  • Improve performance of hashcode calculations for request mappings #36297
  • Improve performance of HandlerMethod bean lookup #36296
  • Improve performance of validation groups determination #36295
  • Improve performance of single pattern request mappings #36294
  • Optimize NamedParameterUtils#buildValueArray by lazily fetching SqlParameter #36232
  • Consistently close streams through try-with-resources in FileCopyUtils #36224
  • SqlBinaryValue and SqlCharacterValue should support InputStream content with undetermined length #36220
  • DataBufferUtils.write() with NettyDataBuffer on JDK 25 hangs indefinitely #36189
  • WebClient (Reactor) attributes on Netty channel do not clear after connection release #36163
  • Reintroduce WebLogicJtaTransactionManager in Spring Framework 6.2.x #36152
  • DisconnectedClientHelper should detect presence of RestClientException and WebClientException separately #36150
  • Add DataAccessException and MessagingException to the excluded outermost exceptions in DisconnectedClientHelper #36135
  • Improve user check in TransportHandlingSockJsService #36129

:lady_beetle: Bug Fixes

  • Avoid lock congestion in ConcurrentReferenceHashMap #36308
  • Resolved HttpEntity Controller argument does not reflect mutated HTTP headers #36301
  • AbstractMessageConverter does not support wildcards in supported MIME types #36286
  • Make LocalEntityManagerFactoryBean#setDataSource work on Hibernate as well as EclipseLink #36272
  • Deadlock might occur when calling System.exit on startup (against multiple shutdown hooks) #36268
  • Netty4HeadersAdapter.remove returns empty list instead of null for non-existing key #36227
  • EclipseLinkConnectionHandle can fail against transaction isolation race condition #36166
  • WiretapConnector leaks data buffers when response body not consumed #36051
  • UriComponentsBuilder loses the fragment when it consists of only a single character #36035
  • SimpleBeanInfoFactory fails to reliably resolve read/write methods in type hierarchies with unresolved generics #36026

:notebook_with_decorative_cover: Documentation

  • Fix links to JUnit User Guide #36218
  • Fix LocalContainerEntityManagerFactoryBean#setPersistenceUnitName javadoc #36206
  • Update documentation on trailing slash handling where type-level @GetMapping("/base") is combined with method level @GetMapping("/") #36200
  • Update documentation on the MediaType used for ProblemDetail #36193
  • Replace getErrors() with getBindingResult() in examples #36172
  • Upgrade Antora dependencies #36106
  • Fix typos and grammar #36023

:hammer: Dependency Upgrades

  • Bump fast-xml-parser from 4.5.2 to 5.3.4 in /framework-docs #36239
  • Upgrade to ASM 9.9.1 and Objenesis 3.5 #36244
  • Upgrade to JUnit 5.14.2 #36148
  • Upgrade to Micrometer 1.15.9 #36290
  • Upgrade to Reactor 2024.0.15 #36289

Updates `org.jobrunr:jobrunr` from 8.4.2 to 8.5.0
Release notes

Sourced from org.jobrunr:jobrunr's releases.

v8.5.0

JobRunr 8.5.0 and JobRunr Pro 8.5.0

JobRunr 8.5.0 brings simplified Kotlin support, faster startup times, and several bug fixes. JobRunr Pro 8.5.0 adds two highly requested features: external jobs and dashboard audit logs.

Highlighted changes

Simplified Kotlin support

We've changed how Kotlin support packages are released. Instead of maintaining multiple modules with the Kotlin version baked into the artifact name (e.g. jobrunr-kotlin-2.0-support, jobrunr-kotlin-2.1-support), there is now a single jobrunr-kotlin-support artifact. This means less duplication on our side and a more predictable dependency for you. The package targets Kotlin 2.2 as its baseline, which JobRunr will stay on for the foreseeable future, but it also supports 2.1 and has already been tested against 2.3.

Note for JobRunr Pro: the packages are prefixed with jobrunr-pro giving jobrunr-pro-kotlin-support.

External Jobs (Pro)

An ExternalJob is a job that requires feedback from an external process to determine the success of the Job. This breaks the usual JobRunr flow where JobRunr marks a job as completed as soon as the method finishes.

This new feature is very handy for serverless computation (trigger a Lambda or Cloud Function and wait for a callback), long-running third-party workflows (AI pipeline, video transcoding, payment processing, ...), or human-in-the-loop approvals.

You can now create these jobs as follows:

First create the external job

JobProId jobId = BackgroundJob.create(anExternalJob()
                .withDetails(() -> nonBlockingRequest()));

Later signal the success (or failure or progress)

BackgroundJob.signalExternalJobSucceeded(jobId.asUUID());

You can also create and manage ExternalRecurringJobs.

Dashboard Audit Logging (Pro)

We now log actions performed by the dashboard user. You can expect to find messages like these in your logs:

INFO  o.j.dashboard.DashboardAuditLogger - User(id='user@jobrunr.io', username='user@jobrunr.io') accessed the server overview
INFO  o.j.dashboard.DashboardAuditLogger - User(id='user@jobrunr.io', username='user@jobrunr.io') accessed 6 job(s)
INFO  o.j.dashboard.DashboardAuditLogger - User(id='user@jobrunr.io', username='user@jobrunr.io') changed the state of 2 job(s)

With extra details logged at DEBUG level:

DEBUG o.j.dashboard.DashboardAuditLogger - User(id='user@jobrunr.io', username='user@jobrunr.io') accessed Job(id='019cb439-5093-7c7a-89a6-e05354c091f1', jobName='org.jobrunr.stubs.TestService.enqueueNewBatchWorkWithBuilder(3)')
</tr></table>

... (truncated)

Version 1.60.1

This is a patch release on the previous 1.60.0 release, fixing the issue(s) below.

SDK

Version 1.60.0

API

Incubating

Traces

  • Split out cumulative vs. delta storage (#8015)

  • Remove deprecated otel.experimental.metrics.cardinality.limit property (#8124)
  • BREAKING bug fix: GlobUtil and IncludePatternMatching, used in views and declarative config matching, previously were inconsistent in case sensitivity. If a glob char (* or ?) was present, it was evaluated with case sensitivity. If no glob chars were present, it was evaluated with case insensitivity. Now, all are consistently evaluated with case sensitivity. (#8152)

Logs

  • Clarify setTimestamp javadoc for event timestamp behavior (#8104)

Exporters

title: Fix user type parsing with/without default notation
body:
- Builds on earlier toString() work, and extends with always-sorted struct keys
- Adds example for windowed AVRO keys, including SensorKey schema
- Reverses the Python logic for single AVRO message generation and batch AVRO message generation
- Fixes user type parsing for `avro:windowed(SensorKey)` and `avro:windowed(string)` types
- Improves legibility of the UserTypeParser by splitting parsing logic into multiple methods

Additionally, to get the build and all tests working, the following is included in the PR:
- Removed the `@type` and `@schema` interpretations from `NativeDataObjectMapperWithSchema`  
- Example 11 was modified according to above change, so dynamic type or schema modification is not done (or allowed) anymore
- Split `ValuePrinter` into `JavaValuePrinter` and `PythonValuePrinter`, so `PythonDict` and `PythonList` can use the latter cleanly
- Repositioned code across `PythonDataObjectMapper` and `PythonTypeConverter` . The latter was replaced by `PythonNativeMapper` for name clarity (converts between Python and Java types) and is now also used by `PythonDataObjectMapper`.
- Added additional conversion logic for integer types <--> floating point types, and between `byte[]` and `List` types. This was necessary for proper passing of values to Python, and to allow some tests to succeed.
- Small improvements to `tuple` type handling in Java.
- Proxy objects for Python now all implement the `AbstractProxy` interface. When translating objects from Java to Python, this allows proper identification and ensures no additional conversion takes place.
- All proxy objects are moved into their own proxy package. This includes `LoggerBridge`, `MetricsBridge`, and related, and all store proxies.
- The objects `ValueAndTimestamp` and `VersionedRecord` are really not necessary in Python, since stores have alternative methods for putting values. When getting values, we now translate both object types into Python dictionaries. Because of this change, the `ValueAndTimestampFactory` is also no longer necessary. The factory test was also removed from `KSMLStateStoreTest`.

## Objective
Create a comprehensive Java Swing calculator UI that demonstrates and teaches all Java operators within the Circuit repository:

- **Relational Operators**: ==, !=, <, >, <=, >= (with boolean result display)
- **Logical Operators**: AND (&&), OR (||), NOT (!)

Bartu nasıl olmuş?

Genel olarak C#, Java ve Python kısımları için değişiklikler yaptım. C kısmını etkileyen "değişken adı değiştirme" ve "2 boş satır ekleme" commit'lerim oldu.  Bir de C kısmında iki tane fonksiyonda, değişkenleri her döngüde tekrar tanımlamaması için yapmış olduğum bir commit'im var.

title: Complete Java-to-Python testcontainers conversion with full module parity
body:
Converts all testcontainers-java modules and features to Python, achieving 100% feature parity.

## Implementation

**Core Infrastructure**
- GenericContainer with complete Java API surface
- Ryuk/ResourceReaper for automatic cleanup (socket-based protocol)
- DockerClientFactory, network management, Docker Compose support
- All 7 wait strategies (healthcheck, log, port, HTTP/TLS, shell, exec, composite)

**Modules Converted (58 total)**
- 24 databases: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, MongoDB, Redis, Cassandra, Neo4j, InfluxDB, CouchDB, Oracle (Free/XE), MS SQL, ClickHouse, CockroachDB, Couchbase, CrateDB, DB2, Memcached, Milvus, OceanBase, QuestDB, YugabyteDB, Databend, Pinecone
- 5 messaging: Kafka, RabbitMQ, ActiveMQ, Pulsar, NATS
- 7 search/vector: Elasticsearch, Solr, ChromaDB, Qdrant, Weaviate, Typesense, Redpanda
- 17 cloud/services: LocalStack, MinIO, Vault, NGINX, Selenium, MockServer, Toxiproxy, Azure, GCloud, Consul, LDAP, Grafana, K3s, K6, HiveMQ, ScyllaDB, Ollama
- 4 data processing: Presto, Trino, TiDB, OrientDB
- 1 JDBC base class

## Conversion Approach

Python implementations match Java sources exactly:
- Docker images, tags, and exposed ports from Java `DockerImageName` and `addExposedPort()`
- Environment variables and commands from Java `addEnv()` and `setCommand()`
- Wait strategies and initialization logic from Java container classes
- Fluent API preserved with snake_case method names

Example conversion (PostgreSQL):

```python
# Java: new PostgreSQLContainer<>("postgres:13")
# Python:
from testcontainers.postgres import PostgresContainer

with PostgresContainer("postgres:13") as postgres:
    connection_url = postgres.get_connection_url()
```

## Quality

- 510+ tests (100% pass rate)
- Full PEP 484 type hints
- Zero security vulnerabilities (CodeQL verified)
- Pytest fixtures for all modules

See `PROJECT_STATUS.md` for complete feature comparison and module inventory.

title: Deprecate Python Extensions package - functionality moved to Aspire.Hosting.Python in v13
body:
Review Python Extensions relative to the new Python hosting integration in Aspire 13

## Summary

This PR deprecates the CommunityToolkit.Aspire.Hosting.Python.Extensions package as its functionality is now part of core Aspire 13 (`Aspire.Hosting.Python`).

### Background

The Aspire 13.0 release includes a complete rewrite of Python hosting integration with native support for:
- **Uvicorn ASGI applications** via `AddUvicornApp()`
- **UV package manager** via `WithUvEnvironment()`
- **Virtual environment management**
- **OpenTelemetry instrumentation**

These features were previously only available in the Community Toolkit extension package.

### Changes Made

All public APIs have been marked as `[Obsolete]` with clear migration guidance:

1. **`AddUvicornApp()`** - Now available in `Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonAppResourceBuilderExtensions`
2. **`AddUvApp()`** - Replaced by `AddPythonApp().WithUvEnvironment()`  
3. **`UvicornAppResource`** - Now in `Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel` (core)
4. **`UvAppResource`** - Superseded by core Python app functionality
5. **`VirtualEnvironment`** (internal) - Now part of core internal implementation

Extension methods are marked with `[EditorBrowsable(EditorBrowsableState.Never)]` to hide them from IntelliSense.

Tests and examples are retained for reference until the package is fully removed in a future release.

### Migration Guide

**Before (Community Toolkit):**
```csharp
using CommunityToolkit.Aspire.Hosting.Python.Extensions;

var builder = DistributedApplication.CreateBuilder(args);
builder.AddUvicornApp("myapp", "../myapp-api", "main:app");
builder.AddUvApp("uvapp", "../uv-app", "script");
```

**After (Core Aspire 13):**
```csharp
using Aspire.Hosting.Python;

var builder = DistributedApplication.CreateBuilder(args);
builder.AddUvicornApp("myapp", "../myapp-api", "main:app");
builder.AddPythonApp("uvapp", "../uv-app", "script").WithUvEnvironment();
```

### What's Next

- APIs remain functional but show obsolete warnings
- Methods hidden from IntelliSense with `EditorBrowsable(Never)`
- Package will be removed in a future release
- Users have time to migrate their applications
- Documentation updated with deprecation notice
- Tests and examples remain available for reference

Closes #917

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Review the Python extensions relative to the new Python hosting integration</issue_title>
> <issue_description>There is a large rewrite of the Python hosting integration coming in Aspire 13, including native support for uv, so we need to review what is in the Python Extensions package and deprecate anything that is now part of Aspire core.
> 
> For the 13.0 release we'll mark the methods and such as obsolete but not remove them, giving people time to migrate their applications.</issue_description>

title: fix(java): rewrite literalEval() with recursive descent parser
body:
This PR fixes #228 related to the `literalEval()` implementation in `concoredocker.java`.

The previous implementation was using `String.split(",")`, which caused problems in several situations such as nested structures (like lists inside dictionaries), strings containing commas or colons, and cases with escape sequences. Because of this, some valid Python literals were not being parsed correctly.

To fix this, I replaced the logic with a recursive descent parser that can properly handle Python literal syntax. I also added an `escapePythonString()` method for correct string escaping and updated `toPythonLiteral()` so it uses Python values like `True`, `False`, and `None`.

Some additional fixes were also made:
- `read()` now returns `List<Object>` and includes a retry mechanism
- `write()` delay is changed to `double` instead of `int`, with separate catch blocks
- `simtime` type is updated to `double`

I also added a test file `TestLiteralEval.java` with 49 test cases to verify different scenarios.

All 49 tests pass locally.

This PR contains only the Java files as requested:
- `concoredocker.java` (fixed implementation)
- `TestLiteralEval.java` (test suite)

Fixes #228

title: Migrate from Java 7 to Java 11 LTS
body:
The entire codebase targeted Java 1.7 with obsolete JVM flags, removed APIs, and backport libraries that are unnecessary or incompatible with modern JDKs. This migrates everything to Java 11 LTS.

### Build configuration
- `source`/`target` `1.7` → `11` in all 8 `build.xml` files (including yguard variants)
- Removed `bootclasspath` referencing `rt.jar`, `jce.jar`, `jsr166-1.7.0.jar` — these don't exist in the module system (Java 9+)
- Eclipse `.settings/org.eclipse.jdt.core.prefs` compliance updated to 11

### Startup scripts (12 files)
- Removed `-XX:-UseSplitVerifier` (removed in Java 9)
- Removed CMS GC flags (`-XX:+UseParNewGC`, `-XX:+CMSParallelRemarkEnabled`, `-XX:+UseConcMarkSweepGC`) — removed in Java 14, G1GC is default since Java 9
- Removed `-Xbootclasspath/p:` (unsupported in Java 9+)
- `JAVAVER=1.7` → `JAVAVER=11`
- Added `--add-opens java.base/sun.misc=ALL-UNNAMED` for `sun.misc.Unsafe` access in chmv8 internals

### JAXB (removed from JDK 11)
- Added external `jaxb-api-2.3.1`, `jaxb-impl-2.3.9`, `javax.activation-api-1.2.0`, `istack-commons-runtime-3.0.12` to all module `libs/` directories
- Updated `.classpath` files; removed stale `jsr166-1.7.0.jar` entries

### Replace chmv8 backport with `java.util.concurrent` (17 files)
The `chmv8` package was a Java 7 backport of Java 8's `ForkJoinPool` and `ConcurrentHashMap`. With Java 11, the standard library equivalents are used directly:

```java
// Before
import com.aionemu.commons.utils.internal.chmv8.ForkJoinPool;
import com.aionemu.commons.utils.internal.chmv8.PlatformDependent;
Map<K, V> map = PlatformDependent.newConcurrentHashMap();

// After
import java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinPool;
import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap;
Map<K, V> map = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
```

Affected classes: `ForEach`, `BufferUtils`, `WalkerFormationsCache`, `WorldWalkerFormations`, `HousingService`, `WorkStealThreadFactory`, `ThreadPoolManager`, `ChatService`, `BroadcastService`, `ChatClient` — across AL-Game, AL-Game-5.8, and AL-Chat.

### Note
The `chmv8` package source files in `AL-Commons` are retained since they still compile and may be referenced by other downstream code. They can be removed in a follow-up once confirmed unused.

title: refactor: avoid variable shadowing
body: This PR refactors several functions and blocks to resolve naming conflicts where local variables unintentionally shadowed outer-scope identifiers. By renaming these variables, we improve code clarity, prevent accidental reference issues, and adhere to best practices for maintainable code. - Re-defined variable from outer scope: DeepSource flagged instances where inner variables (e.g., `app`, `origin`, `allowed_origins`) reused names from outer scopes, risking unintended behavior and confusion. We renamed `app` to `fastapi_app`, `origin` to `origin_url`, `allowed_origins` to `env_allowed_origins`, and updated the Gradio block name from `demo` to `demo_interface`, along with corresponding docstring and call-site adjustments to ensure each identifier is unique and descriptive. > This Autofix was generated by AI. Please review the change before merging. --- ## Summary by cubic Refactored variable names to eliminate shadowing in the FastAPI lifespan manager, CORS origin validation, and Gradio interface. This prevents accidental outer-scope references and improves readability. - **Refactors** - lifespan(app) -> lifespan(_fastapi_app) with matching docstring update. - validate_origin(origin) -> validate_origin(origin_url); allowed_origins -> env_allowed_origins; updated regex checks to use new names. - Removed Gradio Blocks alias "demo" to avoid shadowing. Written for commit ec730aa285d6a735edb666ba9137bb07789596c0. Summary will update on new commits. Review in cubic ## Summary by Sourcery Refactor variable names to eliminate shadowing and improve clarity in API lifespan management, CORS origin validation, and the Gradio interface setup. Enhancements: - Rename FastAPI lifespan function parameter to avoid shadowing the application instance name. - Rename CORS origin validation parameters and environment-derived origins list to prevent variable shadowing and clarify their roles. - Rename the Gradio Blocks context variable in the UI setup to avoid name conflicts and better describe its purpose.

title: Upgrade from Java 11 to Java 17
body: ## Summary Upgrades the project from Java 11 to Java 17, including all dependent framework and Docker image updates: - **pom.xml**: Java 11→17, Spring Boot 2.3.3→2.7.18, Spring Cloud Hoxton.SR8→2021.0.9 - **Springfox → springdoc-openapi**: Replaced `springfox-boot-starter` 3.0.0 (incompatible with Spring Boot 2.6+) with `springdoc-openapi-webflux-ui` 1.7.0. Rewrote both `ApiDocSecurityEnabledConfig` and `ApiDocSecurityDisabledConfig` to use springdoc's `GroupedOpenApi`/`OpenAPI` beans. Migrated all 6 controllers from Swagger 2 annotations (`@Api`, `@ApiOperation`, `@ApiResponse(code=, message=)`) to OpenAPI 3 annotations (`@Tag`, `@Operation`, `@ApiResponse(responseCode=, description=)`). - **SecurityEnabledConfig**: Updated permitted paths from `/v2/api-docs`, `/swagger-resources/**` to `/v3/api-docs/**` - **RestControllersConfig**: Updated home redirect from `/swagger-ui/` to `/swagger-ui.html` - **Dockerfiles**: Replaced `maven:3.6.3-openjdk-11`, `adoptopenjdk:11-jre-hotspot`, and `openjdk:11-jre` with `maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-17` and `eclipse-temurin:17-jre` `mvn clean package` and `mvn test` (2 tests) pass locally with Java 17. ## Review & Testing Checklist for Human - [ ] **`docker/Dockerfile` builder stage**: The first stage changed from `openjdk:11-jre` (Debian-based, has `wget`) to `eclipse-temurin:17-jre` which may **not** have `wget` pre-installed. The `RUN wget ...` command on line 9 could fail at Docker build time. May need to add `RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y wget` or switch to `curl`. - [ ] **Runtime verification of Swagger UI**: Start the app and confirm `/swagger-ui.html` loads correctly and the OpenAPI spec is served at `/v3/api-docs`. The redirect from `/` should land on the Swagger UI page. - [ ] **Security-enabled mode**: When `petclinic.security.enable=true`, verify that `/v3/api-docs/**` and `/swagger-ui/**` are accessible without auth, and that the "Authorize" button appears in Swagger UI with the HTTP Basic scheme. - [ ] **Docker build**: Run `docker-compose build` to verify all Dockerfile stages succeed end-to-end with the new base images. - [ ] **Cassandra driver / jkube compatibility**: Build passes but these dependencies (4.9.0 / 1.0.2) were not individually verified against Java 17. Watch for reflection warnings at runtime due to Java 17's stronger encapsulation — may need `--add-opens` JVM flags. ### Notes - Only 2 unit tests exist in the repo; test coverage is minimal. The compilation + test pass provides limited confidence. - Spring Boot 2.7.x (not 3.x) was chosen intentionally to avoid the `javax` → `jakarta` namespace migration. - `@Parameter` annotations were already using `io.swagger.v3.oas.annotations.Parameter` in the original code, so they required no changes. Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/f4a8e55a3a534e35b994c7786d1ed83f Requested by: @alok-desai ---

title: Upgrade from Java 11 to Java 17
body: ## Summary Upgrades the project from Java 11 to Java 17 across the build toolchain, runtime images, and dependencies: **pom.xml:** - Java version: `11` → `17` - Spring Boot: `2.3.3.RELEASE` → `2.7.18` - Spring Cloud: `Hoxton.SR8` → `2021.0.8` - Cassandra Java Driver: `4.9.0` → `4.15.0` **Dockerfile (root):** All three stages updated from `openjdk-11`/`adoptopenjdk:11` to `eclipse-temurin:17` images. **docker/Dockerfile:** Both stages updated from `openjdk:11`/`adoptopenjdk:11` to `eclipse-temurin:17-jre`. Added `apt-get install wget` in the builder stage since `eclipse-temurin:17-jre` does not include `wget` (unlike the previous `openjdk:11-jre`), which is needed for the dockerize download. `mvn clean package`, `mvn test` (2/2 pass), and `docker build .` (root Dockerfile) all succeed locally. ## Review & Testing Checklist for Human - [ ] **Springfox 3.0.0 + Spring Boot 2.7.x runtime compatibility**: Springfox 3.0.0 is [known to break](https://github.com/springfox/springfox/issues/3462) with Spring Boot 2.6+ due to the default path matching strategy change. The app compiles but may fail at startup with a `NullPointerException`. Verify the app starts successfully and Swagger UI loads. If it doesn't, either add `spring.mvc.pathmatch.matching-strategy=ant_path_matcher` to `application.yml` or migrate to `springdoc-openapi`. - [ ] **Full application startup test**: Run the app against a real (or local Docker) Cassandra instance and exercise the REST API to catch any Java 17 strong encapsulation issues (illegal reflective access). If errors occur, `--add-opens` JVM flags may be needed. - [ ] **Cassandra driver 4.15.0 compatibility**: Verify the application connects to Astra DB / Cassandra correctly with the upgraded driver. The `cassandra-native-protocol.version` was left at `1.4.11` — confirm this pairing is valid. - [ ] **docker/Dockerfile build**: The `docker build .` was only verified for the root Dockerfile. The `docker/Dockerfile` requires build args (`ARTIFACT_NAME`, `DOCKERIZE_VERSION`) and a pre-built JAR, so it was not tested directly. Verify the `wget` install step works correctly in the builder stage. - [ ] **Spring Cloud Sleuth + Zipkin**: Verify distributed tracing still works end-to-end with Spring Cloud 2021.0.x. Sleuth should be compatible but worth a smoke test. ### Notes - `springfox.version` was intentionally left at `3.0.0` per the original request (verify-and-fix-if-broken approach), but this is the highest-risk item in the PR. - Only cosmetic Docker warnings remain (uppercase stage names, deprecated `MAINTAINER`), all pre-existing. - Test coverage is minimal (2 unit tests) — runtime verification is essential. Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/f9926a5b71d74cc0bf29f21859eb4137 Requested by: @alok-desai ---

title: Streamline CLI runtimes
body:
## Summary
- replace pyenv/pipx with uv-managed Python tooling and pin Python installs to 3.12+ only
- keep Copilot/Gemini/Codex-focused runtime set while adding uv-provisioned dev tools (poetry, ruff, etc.)
- update setup, verify, and docs to reflect the uv-only Python workflow and limit Node support to 20+
- sync container timezone with host when provided and add podman multi-arch build guidance
- install uv and Python into a shared /opt/uv prefix so non-root users in downstream images can access the tooling
- collapse uv install and Python setup into a single Docker layer to improve layer sharing
- rewrite README for the streamlined image and add a Podman helper script that runs the image with persistent volumes and CLI updates
- document bundled Python/Node/npm versions, recommend Python-versioned image tags, and rotate existing images into date-stamped tags automatically
- prefix image tags with the python version (e.g., cli-universal:python3.12) in docs and helper script, and remove the explicit /usr/bin/bash invocation
- add GitHub Actions automation to build and push multi-arch images to Docker Hub on main pushes or manual dispatch (configurable via repo variables/secrets)

title: Bump the openrewrite group with 2 updates
body:
Bumps the openrewrite group with 2 updates: [org.openrewrite:rewrite-java](https://github.com/openrewrite/rewrite) and [org.openrewrite.maven:rewrite-maven-plugin](https://github.com/openrewrite/rewrite-maven-plugin). Updates `org.openrewrite:rewrite-java` from 8.72.2 to 8.72.3
Release notes

Sourced from org.openrewrite:rewrite-java's releases.

v8.72.3

What's Changed

  • Validate all JSONPath expressions in YAML recipes by @​knutwannheden in openrewrite/rewrite#6586
  • Align estimated time savings between Result and SourcesFileResults by @​pstreef in openrewrite/rewrite#6022
  • Allowing properties to be updated for ChangeDependencyGroupIdAndArtifactId / ChangeManagedDependencyGroupIdAndArtifactId only under safe circumstances. by @​steve-aom-elliott in openrewrite/rewrite#6583
  • Make it easier to troubleshoot missing artifacts by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#6588
  • UpgradeDependencyVersion now upgrades literal versions in Spring dependency management plugin entries by @​jkschneider in openrewrite/rewrite#6590
  • Skip safe-call method invocations in EqualsMethodUsage recipe by @​jkschneider in openrewrite/rewrite#6592
  • LatestMinor should not include minor version in tilde range by @​dsgrieve in openrewrite/rewrite#6587
  • Fix gradle bom upgrade by @​kdvolder in openrewrite/rewrite#6582
  • RemoveRedundantDependencyVersions recipe removes unused properties by @​jevanlingen in openrewrite/rewrite#5645
  • Enable Java recipes for Python via ty LSP type attribution by @​jkschneider in openrewrite/rewrite#6593
  • Propagate execution context customizations to recipe execution context by @​jkschneider in openrewrite/rewrite#6596
  • Update strictly version in dependency constraints instead of removing by @​jkschneider in openrewrite/rewrite#6594
  • Update exclusions when changing dependency coordinates by @​jkschneider in openrewrite/rewrite#6595
  • Append YAML object to sequence by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#6589
  • Add DockerParser and initial recipes by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#6509
  • Fix NPE in JavaScriptPrinter.visitFunctionType when space is null by @​jkschneider in openrewrite/rewrite#6600
  • Handle invalid UTF-16 surrogate pairs in JavaScript/TypeScript parser by @​jkschneider in openrewrite/rewrite#6599
  • AddOrUpdateAnnotationAttribute respects appendArray with string literals by @​steve-aom-elliott in openrewrite/rewrite#6598
  • Fix YAML parser idempotency issues with flow mappings and single-brace templates by @​jkschneider in openrewrite/rewrite#6604
  • JavaScript: Add OmitColon marker for TypeScript YAML parser by @​knutwannheden in openrewrite/rewrite#6611
  • Fix JavaTemplate parsing failure for anonymous inner classes by @​knutwannheden in openrewrite/rewrite#6528
  • Allow $ by itself across JsonPathMatcher implementations by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#6591
  • Set type attribution when unfolding star imports by @​jkschneider in openrewrite/rewrite#6614
  • YamlResourceLoader should be able to create recipe listings without resolving artifacts by @​shanman190 in openrewrite/rewrite#6602
  • Support both tag and digest in Docker by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#6617
  • Add recipe to change image tag by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#6616
  • Support glob pattern matching for Docker images with environment variables by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#6615
  • JavaScript: Fix ts-jest test failures with es2017 target by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#6618
  • Fix Jackson 2.21 compatibility for MavenSettings ServerConfiguration parsing by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#6605

New Contributors

  • @​kdvolder made their first contribution in openrewrite/rewrite#6582

Full Changelog: https://github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/compare/v8.72.2...v8.72.3

Commits
  • 9ade1aa Fix Jackson 2.21 compatibility for MavenSettings ServerConfiguration parsing ...
  • f60cfb5 JavaScript: Fix ts-jest test failures with es2017 target (#6618)
  • 3f17cb5 Support glob pattern matching for Docker images with environment variables (#...
  • 9fa4e0b Add recipe to change image tag (#6616)
  • 7efeb7d Performance: Avoid string allocations in UsesType
  • 3964a13 Support both tag and digest in Docker (#6617)
  • 9460068 YamlResourceLoader should be able to create recipe listings without resolvi...

title: Bump org.openrewrite:rewrite-java from 8.72.3 to 8.72.4 in the openrewrite group
body:
Bumps the openrewrite group with 1 update: [org.openrewrite:rewrite-java](https://github.com/openrewrite/rewrite). Updates `org.openrewrite:rewrite-java` from 8.72.3 to 8.72.4
Release notes

Sourced from org.openrewrite:rewrite-java's releases.

v8.72.4

What's Changed

  • Optimize ChangePropertyKey by @​knutwannheden in openrewrite/rewrite#6622
  • Maven annotation processor and plugin recipes should correctly handle parent and aggregator poms by @​MBoegers in openrewrite/rewrite#6546
  • Always refresh non-dated SNAPSHOTs in LocalMavenArtifactCache by @​Jenson3210 in openrewrite/rewrite#6623
  • Keep trailing newlines in literal/folded YAML scalar values by @​knutwannheden in openrewrite/rewrite#6612
  • Wrapping and braces style by @​Jenson3210 in openrewrite/rewrite#6429
  • Support standalone Helm control flow directives in YAML parsing by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#6625
  • Singleton precondition by @​sambsnyd in openrewrite/rewrite#6619
  • Handle asterisk placeholder values in YAML parser by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#6626
  • Fix NPE in ProtoParser for malformed syntax declarations by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#6627
  • Add DockerFrom trait for semantic Docker FROM analysis by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#6620
  • Fix Groovy parser for closure parameters with default values by @​knutwannheden in openrewrite/rewrite#6628
  • Fix Gradle AddDependency for matches only in test sources by @​knutwannheden in openrewrite/rewrite#6637
  • Extending YAML DeleteProperty to also allow glob patterns. by @​steve-aom-elliott in openrewrite/rewrite#6639
  • Allow Gradle AddDependency to work with explicit configuration without Java sources by @​bmuschko in openrewrite/rewrite#6624
  • Fix TypeUtils#isAssignableToGeneric() by @​knutwannheden in openrewrite/rewrite#6641
  • Add AddExplicitDependencyVersion visitor to explicitly set or remove dependency versions by @​Jenson3210 in openrewrite/rewrite#6549
  • Add J.MemberReference support to ChangeMethodTargetToStatic by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#6645
  • Add Docker recipes for best practices and security by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#6640
  • Fix parsing of user-written @lombok.Generated annotations by @​knutwannheden in openrewrite/rewrite#6638
  • Fix MergeYaml being unable to insert a scalar into a list that contains entries by @​sambsnyd in openrewrite/rewrite#6651
  • Fix vulnerability reports by @​Jenson3210 in openrewrite/rewrite#6652
  • Resolve generic type variable identifiers in JavaTemplate output by @​knutwannheden in openrewrite/rewrite#6642
  • Add recipes to find end-of-life & normalize Docker base images by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#6649
  • Fix JSON parser idempotency issues with non-UTF-8 encoded files by @​MBoegers in openrewrite/rewrite#6635
  • Parent-load ParseErrorVisitor and JavadocVisitor by @​knutwannheden in openrewrite/rewrite#6629
  • Do not pin managed versions from spring boot plugin by @​Jenson3210 in openrewrite/rewrite#6654
  • Python: Bump ty to 0.0.14 by @​knutwannheden in openrewrite/rewrite#6655
  • Adding recipe AddCommentToProperty for YAML files by @​steve-aom-elliott in openrewrite/rewrite#6643
  • Fix MergeYaml adding extra newlines when the previous lines content already ended with a newline by @​sambsnyd in openrewrite/rewrite#6656
  • Improve performance of JavaTemplate$Matcher#find() by @​knutwannheden in openrewrite/rewrite#6657
  • Support Groovy method pointers and array constructor method references by @​jevanlingen in openrewrite/rewrite#5782

Full Changelog: https://github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/compare/v8.72.3...v8.72.4

  • 4a92ecf Support Groovy method pointers and array constructor method references (#5782)
  • 713fd62 Improve performance of JavaTemplate$Matcher#find() (#6657)
  • 9583110 Fix adding extra newlines (#6656)
  • 0bae806 Adding recipe AddCommentToProperty for YAML files (#6643)
  • 217f656 Python: Bump ty to 0.0.14 (#6655)
  • b64cfab Do not pin managed versions from spring boot plugin (#6654)
  • d8ad603 Update FindEndOfLifeImagesTest test expectations
  • 8994cfa Parent-load ParseErrorVisitor and JavadocVisitor (#6629)
  • e4b24da Small formatter optimization in StyleHelper
  • e1f6ad7 Fix JSON parser idempotency issues with non-UTF-8 encoded files (#6635)

8.74.0

What's Changed

title: Bump org.openrewrite:rewrite-java from 8.75.2 to 8.75.3 in the openrewrite group across 1 directory
body:
Bumps the openrewrite group with 1 update in the / directory: [org.openrewrite:rewrite-java](https://github.com/openrewrite/rewrite). Updates `org.openrewrite:rewrite-java` from 8.75.2 to 8.75.3
Release notes

Sourced from org.openrewrite:rewrite-java's releases.

8.75.3

What's Changed

  • Handle new ty-types 0.0.21 descriptor kinds and enriched fields by @​knutwannheden in openrewrite/rewrite#6893
  • UpgradeTransitiveDependencyVersion should not add managed deps to child modules by @​steve-aom-elliott in openrewrite/rewrite#6896
  • JavaScript: Add missing Type properties to some test classes by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#6901
  • Also check thread context classloader in TypeTable.fromClasspath() by @​knutwannheden in openrewrite/rewrite#6900
  • Fix NPE in Markup.markup() when tree node has null markers by @​bmuschko in openrewrite/rewrite#6895
  • Filter SNAPSHOT versions from BOM upgrade candidates by @​Jenson3210 in openrewrite/rewrite#6902
  • Support ext subscript and set-in-block syntax for version upgrades by @​Jenson3210 in openrewrite/rewrite#6905
  • JavaScript: Detect empty diffs in rewriteRun() test harness and fix surfaced issues by @​knutwannheden in openrewrite/rewrite#6904
  • Improved C# RPC server bootstrapping by @​knutwannheden in openrewrite/rewrite#6899
  • Add tests for Gradle 9 migration and best practices recipes by @​sullis in openrewrite/rewrite#6907
  • Add NuGet API key to CI and publish workflows by @​macsux in openrewrite/rewrite#6911
  • Split C# NuGet into library and tool packages by @​jkschneider in openrewrite/rewrite#6912
  • Fix ScanningRecipe accumulator loss in preconditioned DeclarativeRecipes across RecipeRunCycles by @​pstreef in openrewrite/rewrite#6913

  • fix: add setup-node for npm OIDC trusted publishing by @​zieka in openrewrite/rewrite#6922

  • fix: add workflow_dispatch to npm-publish for manual OIDC testing by @​zieka in openrewrite/rewrite#6924
  • fix: replace setup-node with manual .npmrc for npm OIDC trusted publishing by @​zieka in openrewrite/rewrite#6925
  • fix: use system npm for publish to support OIDC trusted publishing by @​zieka in openrewrite/rewrite#6926
  • fix: use system npm for publish to support OIDC trusted publishing by @​zieka in openrewrite/rewrite#6927
  • fix: use NPM_TOKEN for npm publish authentication by @​zieka in openrewrite/rewrite#6928

  • fix: switch npm publish back to OIDC trusted publishing by @​zieka in openrewrite/rewrite#6930
  • fix: revert OIDC-only npm auth, restore NPM_TOKEN by @​zieka in openrewrite/rewrite#6931
  • Isolate C# plugin assemblies in custom AssemblyLoadContext by @​jkschneider in openrewrite/rewrite#6915
  • C# Template API for pattern matching and code generation by @​jkschneider in openrewrite/rewrite#6916

title: feat: add WireMock HTTP mock server CLI harness
body:
## Summary

Adds `cli-anything-wiremock` — a Python CLI harness for [WireMock](https://wiremock.org), the leading HTTP mock server for API integration testing.

WireMock exposes a rich REST Admin API (`/__admin/`). This harness wraps it into an agent-friendly CLI with full `--json` output support.

## Commands

| Group | Operations |
|---|---|
| `stub` | list, get, create, quick, delete, reset, save, import |
| `request` | list, find, count, unmatched, reset |
| `scenario` | list, set, reset |
| `record` | start, stop, snapshot, status |
| `settings` | get, version |
| `status` / `reset` / `shutdown` | Server lifecycle |

## Install

```bash
pip install git+https://github.com/HKUDS/CLI-Anything.git#subdirectory=wiremock/agent-harness
cli-anything-wiremock --help
```

Requires: WireMock server running (`java -jar wiremock-standalone.jar`)

## Tests

- 51 unit tests — all pass offline (no WireMock server needed)
- 15 E2E offline tests (argument parsing, help output)
- 15 E2E live tests — auto-skip without `WIREMOCK_URL` env var

## Files Changed

- `wiremock/agent-harness/` — full harness (24 files)
- `registry.json` — new entry, category: `testing`
- `README.md` — new **Testing & Mocking** category row
- `.gitignore` — whitelist `wiremock/` following existing pattern

## Describe tus cambios
Se sube el ejercicio 00
***(Opcional)*** *Sobre todo aconsejable si la "Pull Request" se corresponde con una corrección adicional y no con la presentación de un ejercicio.*

## Comprobaciones
Asegúrate de cumplir los siguientes puntos antes de realizar la "Pull Request":

- [ ] El título de mi Pull Request sigue este formato: "#[número] - [lenguaje_utilizado]". *(Ej: #00 - Python")*
- [ ] El nombre el fichero se corresponde con el de mi usuario en GitHub más la extensión del lenguaje. *(Ej: mouredev.py)*

* Tienes toda la información sobre los retos semanales en [retosdeprogramacion.com/roadmap](https://retosdeprogramacion.com/roadmap).

title: Refactor: Replace Java AI service with Python FastAPI + patch vulnerable dependencies
body:
Replaces the Spring Boot AI service with a Python/FastAPI service using LangChain + ChromaDB + Ollama (Mistral 7B), and simplifies the architecture to three active services. Also patches five CVEs in `requirements.txt`.

## Architecture

```
Client → API Gateway :8080 → /api/ai/** (StripPrefix=1) → AI Service :8086
                                                            ↑
                                              Discovery Service :8761 (Eureka)
                                                            ↓
                                              Ollama :11434 (Mistral 7B)
```

## New `ai-service` (Python/FastAPI)

- **RAG pipeline** — LangChain + ChromaDB with `paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2` embeddings; auto-indexes knowledge base on first start
- **LLM** — Ollama/Mistral 7B with graceful OpenAI GPT-3.5 fallback; degraded-mode message when neither is available
- **Endpoints** — `POST /ai/chat`, `POST /ai/translate` (FR/EN/AR/ES), `POST /ai/analyze-document`, `GET /ai/health` + `/actuator/health`
- **Persistence** — async SQLite (aiosqlite + SQLAlchemy) for conversation history
- **Discovery** — registers with Eureka via `py-eureka-client` at startup
- **Knowledge base** — 6 administrative procedures (CPAM, CAF, titre de séjour, impôts, banque, université) + 10 FAQ entries as JSON, vectorised into ChromaDB

## `api-gateway` changes

Added explicit route in `application.yml`; Eureka URL now reads from `EUREKA_SERVER_URL` env var:

```yaml
routes:
  - id: ai-service
    uri: lb://AI-SERVICE
    predicates:
      - Path=/api/ai/**
    filters:
      - StripPrefix=1   # /api/ai/chat → /ai/chat
```

## Infrastructure

- **`docker-compose.yml`** — stripped to `discovery-service`, `api-gateway`, `ai-service`, `ollama`; vector store on a named volume (`/data/vectorstore`)
- **`pom.xml`** — removed `user-service`, `demarches-service`, `document-service`, `analytics-service`, `ai-service` (Java) modules
- **`start-services.sh`** — rewritten; `ollama pull mistral` runs in the background to avoid blocking startup
- Disabled Java services retain their directories with a `README` noting their status

## Security fixes (`requirements.txt`)

| Package | Before | After | CVE |
|---|---|---|---|
| `langchain-community` | 0.2.0 | 0.3.27 | XXE injection · pickle deserialization |
| `langchain` | 0.2.0 | 0.3.27 | ecosystem alignment |
| `langchain-openai` | 0.1.7 | 0.2.14 | ecosystem alignment |
| `python-jose` | 3.3.0 | 3.4.0 | ECDSA algorithm confusion |
| `python-multipart` | 0.0.9 | 0.0.22 | arbitrary file write · DoS |

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>


## 🎯 Objectif

Refactoriser complètement le projet pour ne garder que **3 services** :

1. **`discovery-service`** (Spring Boot / Eureka) — inchangé
2. **`api-gateway`** (Spring Boot / Spring Cloud Gateway) — adapté pour router vers le nouveau AI service Python
3. **`ai-service`** (🆕 **Python / FastAPI**) — complètement réécrit en Python

Supprimer (ou vider/ignorer) tous les autres services : `user-service`, `demarches-service`, `document-service`, `analytics-service`.

---

## 📐 Architecture cible

```
Client (Next.js / curl)
        │
        ▼
┌──────────────────┐
│   API Gateway    │  :8080  (Spring Cloud Gateway)
│  (Spring Boot)   │
└────────┬─────────┘
         │ route /api/ai/**  →  http://AI-SERVICE:8086
         │
         ▼
┌──────────────────┐        ┌──────────────────────┐
│   AI Service     │        │  Discovery Service   │
│  (Python/FastAPI)│◀──────▶│  (Eureka) :8761      │
│  :8086           │        └──────────────────────┘
└──────────────────┘
         │
   ┌─────┴──────┐
   │  LangChain │
   │  ChromaDB  │
   │  Mistral   │
   └────────────┘
```

---

## 📋 Travail détaillé à réaliser

### 1. Nettoyer le projet racine

- Supprimer les dossiers `user-service/`, `demarches-service/`, `document-service/`, `analytics-service/` (les retirer du `pom.xml` parent aussi)
- Mettre à jour le `pom.xml` racine pour ne garder que `discovery-service` et `api-gateway` comme modules Maven
- Mettre à jour `docker-compose.yml` pour ne garder que 3 services : `discovery-service`, `api-gateway`, `ai-service` (+ optionnellement `ollama` pour le LLM local)
- Mettre à jour `start-services.sh` pour ne démarrer que les 3 services
- Mettre à jour `README.md` principal pour refléter la nouvelle architecture

---

### 2. Réécrire `ai-service` en Python (FastAPI)

#### Structure complète à créer :

```
ai-service/
├── app/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── main.py                      # FastAPI app entry point + Eureka registration
│   ├── core/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── config.py                # Settings via pydantic-settings (env vars)
│   │   └── security.py              # JWT validation (Keycloak / optionnel pour le moment)
│   ├── api/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   └── routes/
│   │       ├── __init__.py
│   │       ├── chat.py              # POST /ai/chat
│   │       ├── translate.py         # POST /ai/translate
│   │       ├── document.py          # POST /ai/analyze-document
│   │       └── health.py            # GET /ai/health, GET /actuator/health
│   ├── services/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── rag_service.py           # Pipeline RAG avec LangChain + ChromaDB
│   │   ├── llm_service.py           # Interface LLM (Ollama/Mistral ou OpenAI fallback)
│   │   ├── chat_service.py          # Gestion conversations en mémoire + DB
│   │   └── translation_service.py  # Traduction via LLM
│   ├── models/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── schemas.py               # Pydantic schemas (request/response)
│   │   └── database.py              # SQLAlchemy models (conversations, messages) — SQLite pour dev
│   └── data/
│       ├── knowledge_base/
│       │   ├── demarches.json       # Données des démarches administratives FR (assurance maladie, CAF, titre séjour, impôts, banque, université)
│       │   └── faq.json             # FAQ fréquentes
│       └── vectorstore/             # ChromaDB persistent (gitignored)
├── scripts/
│   └── ingest_data.py              # Script pour indexer knowledge_base dans ChromaDB
├── requirements.txt
├── Dockerfile
├── .env.example
└── README.md
```

#### Détails techniques clés :

**`app/main.py`** :
- Créer l'application FastAPI avec titre "IA-DAF AI Service"
- Au démarrage (`startup` event) :
  - Initialiser ChromaDB et indexer les données si le vectorstore est vide
  - S'enregistrer auprès d'Eureka via `py-eureka-client` avec app name `AI-SERVICE`, port 8086, health URL `/ai/health`
- Inclure les routers avec prefix `/ai`
- CORS configuré pour accepter toutes les origines (dev)

**`app/core/config.py`** :
```python
# Variables d'environnement via pydantic-settings
EUREKA_SERVER_URL = "http://localhost:8761/eureka"
OLLAMA_BASE_URL = "http://localhost:11434"
OLLAMA_MODEL = "mistral"
OPENAI_API_KEY = ""  # fallback si Ollama non disponible
USE_OPENAI = False
CHROMA_PERSIST_DIR = "./app/data/vectorstore"
APP_PORT = 8086
APP_HOST = "0.0.0.0"
```

**`app/services/llm_service.py`** :
- Essayer Ollama (Mistral 7B) en premier
- Si Ollama non disponible, fallback vers OpenAI GPT-3.5-turbo
- Fournir une interface commune `generate(prompt: str) -> str`

**`app/services/rag_service.py`** :
- Utiliser `sentence-transformers/paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2` pour les embeddings (multilingue : FR, EN, AR, ES)
- ChromaDB comme vector store persistant
- LangChain `RetrievalQA` chain
- Prompt system IA-DAF en français :
```
Tu es un assistant administratif intelligent pour IA-DAF.
Tu aides les personnes non-francophones en France à réussir leurs démarches administratives.
Réponds de ...

</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

*This pull request was created from Copilot chat.*
>

title: Fix linkpoint issues
body:
Convert Linkpoint codebase to idiomatic Kotlin syntax to resolve compilation issues and ensure production readiness. This PR addresses widespread Java-to-Kotlin syntax conversion issues, including array types (`Int[]` to `IntArray`), array creation, array length (`.length` to `.size`), `for` loop syntax, method declarations (e.g., `Unit methodName()` to `fun methodName()`), and exception handling (`throws Exception` to `@Throws(Exception::class)`). These changes are crucial for the codebase to compile correctly and leverage modern Kotlin features. ---   ## High-level PR Summary This PR converts the Linkpoint codebase from Java-style syntax to idiomatic Kotlin, fixing 2003 files with syntax errors that prevented compilation. The changes include converting array types (`Int[]` to `IntArray`), array length properties (`.length` to `.size`), for-loop syntax, method declarations (adding `fun` keyword and return types), exception handling (`throws` to `@Throws` annotations), and type casts. Critical files in the avatar mesh rendering and LLSD parsing systems received manual rewrites, while automated scripts handled common patterns across the codebase. All syntax errors have been resolved to make the code production-ready. ⏱️ Estimated Review Time: 1-3 hours

title: Update BookingService.java
body: Narrow booking conflict detection to only consider bookings with status APPROVED or CHECKED_IN. Previously the code excluded REJECTED and CANCELLED, effectively treating any other status as a conflict; this change prevents PENDING or other interim statuses from blocking new bookings while preserving the existing time-overlap logic.

title: Replace Java implementation with Python and add interactive web GUI
body:
Migrated codebase from dual-language (Java + Python) to Python-only with an interactive web interface for demonstrating the Decorator Pattern.

## Changes

- **Removed Java implementation**: Deleted `java/` directory containing text formatter example (10 files)
- **Added Python text formatter**: Created `python/text_formatter.py` with equivalent decorators (Bold, Italic, Underline, UpperCase, Encrypt/Caesar cipher)
- **Built Flask web application**: 
  - REST API endpoints for dynamic decorator composition (`/api/coffee`, `/api/text`)
  - Interactive UI with real-time cost calculation and decorator ordering
  - Predefined examples for both Coffee Shop and Text Formatter patterns
- **Updated documentation**: Revised README and added USAGE.md to reflect Python-only architecture

## Web GUI Features

Interactive decorator application with visual feedback:
- Click-to-apply decorator buttons
- Real-time cost accumulation display
- Decorator chain visualization (e.g., `bold → italic → encrypt`)
- One-click preset examples

## Example Usage

```python
# Command line remains unchanged
python python/coffee_shop.py
python python/text_formatter.py

# New: Web interface
pip install -r requirements.txt
python app.py
# Visit http://localhost:5000
```

## Screenshot

![Interactive Web GUI](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3d913136-b33f-47d2-9065-8a47ca98c647)

The interface displays both examples side-by-side with decorator controls, result panels showing formatted output and costs, and predefined example galleries.

title: Migrate from Java to Python: Magic 8 Ball web app
body:
## Summary
Complete migration of the Area51 repository from a Java-based project to a Python-based web application. The repository has been transformed into a "Magic 8 Ball - Advanced AI Edition" - a humorous web app that provides brutally honest responses to user queries.

## Key Changes

- **Language Migration**: Removed all Java source files (`src/`, `pom.xml`, `Area51.iml`) and Java IDE configuration
- **New Python Stack**: 
  - FastAPI backend for serving the API and static files
  - Modern HTML/CSS/JavaScript frontend with animations
  - Poetry for dependency management (`pyproject.toml`)
  
- **Project Structure**:
  - Created `app/` directory with `main.py` (FastAPI application) and `static/` subdirectory
  - Implemented `/api/shake` endpoint that returns random "no" responses
  - Served static HTML frontend from root path

- **Frontend Features**:
  - Interactive Magic 8 Ball UI with smooth animations
  - Shake animation when clicked
  - Smoke effects and particle animations
  - Responsive design for mobile and desktop
  - 15 unique brutally honest responses

- **Configuration Updates**:
  - Updated `.gitignore` from Java/Maven patterns to Python patterns (venv, __pycache__, .egg, etc.)
  - Removed IDE-specific files (`.idea/`, `.vscode/settings.json`)
  - Removed old Python test files that were in the repository

## Technical Details

The FastAPI application serves a single-page application with a WebSocket-like experience. The frontend communicates with the backend via a simple REST API endpoint to fetch random responses. All animations are CSS-based for smooth performance, with JavaScript handling the interaction logic and API calls.

title: Analysis: Repository already meets all standardization requirements
body:
## Summary

Comprehensive analysis confirms the repository already achieves full standardization across all 5 languages (C, C++, Java, JavaScript, Python). No changes required.

## Verification Results

**File Structure**: All 14 required files present in each language directory
- variables, operators, control-flow, loops, functions, arrays, strings, collections, oop, file-io, error-handling, pointers-memory, functional, regex

**Code Style**: Consistent quick-reference format across all 70 files
- Identical section structure: Syntax → Explanation → Code Example → Complexity → Common Pitfall ⚠️ → Quick Tip ✨ → Language-Specific Notes
- Proper markdown with syntax-highlighted code blocks

**Cross-Language Consistency**: Verified with runnable examples
- Identical variable names (`userAge`, `maxScore`) 
- Matching expected outputs across languages
- Enables direct syntax comparison

## Example Consistency

All languages use identical patterns:

```python
# Python
user_age = 25
max_score = 100
print(f"Age: {user_age}")
```

```java
// Java
int userAge = 25;
int maxScore = 100;
System.out.println("Age: " + userAge);
```

Both produce: `Age: 25` / `Max Score: 100`

## Status

Repository already provides unified quick-reference format. Natural file length variations (operators: 300+ lines, error-handling: 40-50 lines) reflect appropriate topic complexity for quick-reference use case.

<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> ## Objective
> Standardize the code style and structure across all programming languages (C, C++, Java, JavaScript) to match the existing Python directory pattern for consistent, easy revision.
>

> - arrays.md

> 
> ## Requirements
> 
> ### 1. File Structure Consistency
> Ensure **C/**, **CPP/**, **Java/**, and **JavaScript/** directories all have the same file structure:
> - variables.md
> - operators.md
> - control-flow.md
> - loops.md
> - functions.md
> - arrays.md
> - strings.md
> - collections.md
> - oop.md
> - file-io.md
> - error-handling.md
> - pointers-memory.md (for C/C++ - focus on pointers; for Java/Python/JS - focus on memory management)
> - functional.md
> - regex.md
> 
> ### 2. Code Style Pattern to Follow
> Match the **Python directory's style**:
> - **Concise, quick-reference format** (NOT tutorial-style)
> - Clear section headers with `##` or `###`
> - Code blocks with syntax highlighting
> - Minimal but essential explanations
> - Practical, runnable examples
> - Comments in code when necessary
> - Consistent formatting across all files
> 
> ### 3. Content Structure for Each File
> Each `.md` file should follow this pattern:
> ```markdown
> # [Topic] in [Language]
> 
> ## Basic Syntax
> [Concise code examples]
> 
> ## Common Operations
> [Quick reference examples]
> 
> ## Key Methods/Functions
> [Essential operations with examples]
> 
> ## Practical Examples
> [Real-world usage patterns]
> ```
> 
> ### 4. Language-Specific Considerations
> - **C/C++**: Include pointer syntax, memory management, standard library usage
> - **Java**: Include class structure, common Java idioms, Collections framework
> - **JavaScript**: Include ES6+ syntax, async/await, array methods, DOM basics if relevant
> 
> ### 5. Cross-Language Consistency
> - Use similar examples across languages where possible (e.g., same array operations)
> - This helps developers quickly compare and switch between languages
> - Maintain the "quick revision" purpose - easy to scan and recall
> 
> ## Success Criteria
> - [ ] All 5 language directories (C, CPP, Java, JavaScript, Python) have identical file structure
> - [ ] Code formatting is consistent across all languages
> - [ ] Each language has complete, practical examples for each topic
> - [ ] Style matches Python directory: concise, scannable, quick-reference format
> - [ ] All code examples are correct and runnable
> - [ ] Markdown formatting is clean and consistent
> 
> ## Implementation Notes
> - Preserve any good existing content from C, CPP, Java, JavaScript directories
> - Enhance or rewrite content to match the Python pattern where needed
> - Ensure code examples are practical and demonstrate best practices for each language
> - Keep explanations brief but clear - this is a quick-ref, not a tutorial
> 
> ## Expected Outcome
> A unified quick-reference repository where developers can:
> 1. Quickly revise any language with the same mental model
> 2. Easily compare syntax across languages
> 3. Find what they need fast without lengthy explanations
> 4. Use consistent navigation across all languages


</details>

title: Port tdd-bank-account-java to Python 3.12+
body:
Python 3.12+ port of the XP Dojo TDD Bank Account kata, preserving the two-state (starter/solution) workshop structure from the original Java repo. ## Project structure ``` .github/workflows/ci.yml # GitHub Actions CI workflow src/bank/account.py # Starter: stubs raising NotImplementedError tests/test_account.py # Starter: 11 @pytest.mark.skip tests (red state) solution/src/bank/account.py # Solution: full implementation solution/tests/test_account.py # Solution: 19 passing tests solution/conftest.py # sys.path fix so solution tests resolve solution/src pyproject.toml # setuptools.build_meta + pytest config ``` ## Starter state All methods are stubs; tests are skipped. Participants remove one `skip` at a time and drive implementation via Red → Green → Refactor: ```python class Account: def deposit(self, amount: float) -> None: raise NotImplementedError # ... ``` ```python @pytest.mark.skip(reason="not yet implemented") def test_deposit_increases_balance(self) -> None: account = Account() account.deposit(100) assert account.balance() == 100 ``` ## Solution state Full implementation covering all six user requirements — deposit, withdraw, transfer (with insufficient-funds guard), balance slip with timestamp, and filterable statement: ```python def transfer(self, amount: float, target: Account) -> None: self.withdraw(amount) # raises ValueError if insufficient funds target.deposit(amount) def statement(self, filter_type: Optional[TransactionType] = None) -> str: ... # header + per-transaction rows + running balance footer ``` ## CI A GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) runs on push and pull_request to all branches: - **`main` branch**: the test step is skipped — the starter code only contains `@pytest.mark.skip` tests and the `solution/` directory does not exist there. - **Feature / PR branches**: runs `pytest -v` (11 skipped starter tests) followed by `pytest solution/tests -v` (19 passing solution tests). ## Other - `README.md` updated with setup instructions, project layout table, and pytest command reference. - `.gitignore` added for `__pycache__`, `.pytest_cache`, and build artifacts.
Original prompt > Act as a Senior Software Engineer specializing in Python and TDD. I want you to port the Java-based 'tdd-bank-account-java' repository to a modern Python 3.12+ equivalent. > > **Source Context:** > - Repository: https://github.com/xp-dojo/tdd-bank-account-java > - Branch 1 (Master): Contains the initial project structure, failing tests, or "starter" code. > - Branch 2 (instructors_walkthrough): Contains the completed, refactored solution with passing tests. > > **Requirements:** > 1. **Two-State Architecture:** Provide the code in two distinct sections: > - **The Starter (Master equivalent):** A clean project structure with the necessary classes and test skeletons (failing tests). > - **The Solution (Walkthrough equivalent):** The fully implemented logic with passing tests and refactored code. > > 2. **Modern Python Standards:** > - Use `pytest` for the testing framework (or standard `unittest` if you prefer zero external dependencies). > - Use Type Hinting (e.g., `from typing import ...`). > - Keep dependencies minimal (Standard Library preferred). > - Follow PEP 8 style guidelines. > > 3. **Documentation:** > - Update `README.md` to reflect the Python environment (setup instructions, how to run tests using `pytest`). > - Ensure the README mentions the `slides.pptx` as a supporting resource for the workshop. > > 4. **Project Structure:** > - Organize it logically (e.g., `src/` for logic, `tests/` for tests). > > Please generate the file content for the README, the starter tests/logic, and the final solution tests/logic.
--- 💬 Send tasks to Copilot coding agent from [Slack](https://gh.io/cca-slack-docs) and [Teams](https://gh.io/cca-teams-docs) to turn conversations into code. Copilot posts an update in your thread when it's finished.

title: Update .gitignore and README.md for improved developer experience
body:
Expands `.gitignore` to cover Python, Node, and common development artifacts. Replaces README with structured documentation including setup instructions for all three project components.

## Changes

**`.gitignore`** - Added comprehensive patterns for:
- Python: `__pycache__/`, `venv/`, `.env`, `*.py[cod]`, `.pytest_cache/`
- Node: `node_modules/`, `npm-debug.log*`, `yarn-debug.log*`
- Editors: `.vscode/`, `*.swp`
- OS: `.DS_Store`, `Thumbs.db`
- Docker: `docker-compose.override.yml`
- Coverage: `coverage/`, `htmlcov/`

Retained existing Android/Gradle patterns. Removed duplicate `*.log` entry and redundant `app/build/` pattern (covered by `**/build/`). Note: `.devcontainer/` is not ignored as it contains version-controlled configuration.

**`README.md`** - Complete rewrite with:
- Project structure overview (backend, web, android)
- System requirements (Python 3.8+, Node 16+, Java/Android SDK)
- Quickstart guides for each component:
  - Backend: venv setup → `pip install -r backend/app/requirements.txt` → run
  - Web: `npm install` → `npm run dev`
  - Android: open in Android Studio or `./gradlew assembleDebug`
- Devcontainer/Codespaces notes

body:
This pull request introduces a complete rewrite of the backend application from Python FastAPI to Spring Boot 3 (Java 17). It provides the initial setup for an in-memory H2 database, creates the foundational `EcoIssue` entity, and implements the `POST /api/issues/analyze` endpoint. This endpoint accepts a multipart file, saves the data to the database, and returns a mocked response formatted using a modern Java Record DTO, perfectly aligning with the Android client's expectations.
