title: Rewrite Amanoform in Java for enterprise-grade comedy
body:
## Summary

- **Full rewrite from Python to Java 17** — replaces Click/Playwright/Rich with Picocli/Selenium WebDriver/ANSI codes, and swaps `pyproject.toml` for a 150-line Maven `pom.xml`
- **Functionally identical** — same commands (`init`, `plan`, `apply`, `destroy`), same `.af` config format, same `amanoform.state` JSON format, same browser automation logic
- **Significantly more enterprise** — source files doubled (7 → 15), lines of code quadrupled (~500 → ~1,900), class names like `AWSManagementConsoleSessionProvider` and `EC2InstanceManualProvisioningHandler`, `ResourceHandlerRegistryFactory`, checked exceptions around `Thread.sleep()`, and a 24MB uber-JAR

### Migration stats

| Metric | Python | Java | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Source files | 7 | 15 | +114% |
| Lines of code | ~500 | ~1,900 | +280% |
| Build config lines | 22 (TOML) | 150 (XML) | +582% |
| Longest class name | 18 chars | 45 chars | +150% |
| Artifact size | ~10KB | 24MB | +240,000% |

## Test plan

- [x] `mvn clean package` builds successfully
- [x] `java -jar target/amanoform-0.1.0.jar --help` displays CLI help
- [x] `java -jar target/amanoform-0.1.0.jar plan -c examples/main.af` parses config and shows plan
- [ ] `amanoform init` validates ChromeDriver availability
- [ ] `amanoform apply --no-headless` opens browser and provisions EC2 (requires AWS creds)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

- [x] Live tests: Binance, OKX, Kraken, Bybit, Coinbase Exchange

title: Fix test failure formatting in PR comments - preserve newlines for code comparisons
body:
Test failures in PR comments were unreadable due to newline removal and markdown escaping. Java code comparisons appeared as single-line escaped strings in table cells.

## Changes

**Parse from stacktrace text instead of XML attribute**
- XML attributes normalize whitespace per spec - newlines become spaces
- Text content of `<failure>` element preserves formatting
- Added `parse_source` field that prefers text content when available

**New `parse_assertion_error()` function**
- Extracts expected/actual from `expected: <...> but was: <...>` pattern
- Handles alternative `Expected: ... Actual: ...` format
- Returns `None` for non-parseable errors (NPE, etc.)

**Sectioned layout replaces table format**
- Each test gets numbered section with class.method
- Expected/Actual in separate Java code blocks
- Stack traces in collapsible `<details>`
- Removed `escape_markdown()` - code blocks handle it naturally

## Example Output

**Before:**
```
| Test | Message |
| `test_import_migration` | expected: \<import org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.\*;\> but was: \<import org.junit.Assert.\*;\> |
```

**After:**
```markdown
### 1. MigrationTest.test_import_migration

**Expected:**
```java
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;
```

**Actual:**
```java
import org.junit.Assert.*;
```
```

## Files Changed
- `.github/scripts/extract_test_failures.py` - Complete rewrite of formatting logic
- `.gitignore` - Add Python cache exclusions

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> ## Problem
> 
> Die Formatierung der Testergebnisse im PR-Kommentar ist schwer lesbar. Die `escape_markdown()` Funktion entfernt alle Newlines und quetscht die Fehlermeldungen in eine Tabellenzeile, was besonders bei Java-Code-Vergleichen (expected vs. actual) unlesbar wird.
> 
> ## Aktuelles Ergebnis (schlecht lesbar)
> 
> ```markdown
> | Test | Class | Message |
> |------|-------|---------|
> | `handles\_wildcard\_static\_imports` | `MigrationEdgeCasesTest` | expected: \<package test; import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.\*; import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test; public class MyTest { @Test public void testAssertions() { assertEquals(42, 42); assertTrue(true); assertFalse(false); } } \> but was: \<package test; import static org.junit.Assert.\*; ... |
> ```
> 
> ## Gewünschtes Ergebnis (gut lesbar)
> 
> ```markdown
> ## ❌ Failed Tests Details
> 
> <!-- test-failures-comment -->
> 
> **8 test(s) failed**
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 1. MigrationEdgeCasesTest.handles_wildcard_static_imports
> 
> **Expected:**
> ```java
> package test;
> import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;
> 
> import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
> 
> public class MyTest {
>     @Test
>     public void testAssertions() {
>         assertEquals(42, 42);
>     }
> }
> ```
> 
> **Actual:**
> ```java
> package test;
> import static org.junit.Assert.*;
> 
> import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
> 
> public class MyTest {
>     @Test
>     public void testAssertions() {
>         assertEquals(42, 42);
>     }
> }
> ```
> 
> <details>
> <summary>📋 Full Stack Trace</summary>
> 
> ```
> org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError: 
> expected: <...> but was: <...>
>     at org.junit.jupiter.api.AssertionFailureBuilder.build(...)
>     ...
> ```
> 
> </details>
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 2. MigrationAssertionsTest.migrates_static_import_wildcard
> 
> ...
> ```
> 
> ## Änderungen an `.github/scripts/extract_test_failures.py`
> 
> ### 1. Neue Funktion zum Parsen von expected/actual
> 
> ```python
> def parse_assertion_error(message):
>     """Parse expected/actual from assertion error message."""
>     # Pattern: "expected: <...> but was: <...>"
>     import re
>     match = re.search(r'expected:\s*<(.*)>\s*but was:\s*<(.*)>', message, re.DOTALL)
>     if match:
>         return {
>             'expected': match.group(1).strip(),
>             'actual': match.group(2).strip()
>         }
>     return None
> ```
> 
> ### 2. Neue Formatierung (keine Tabelle, separate Abschnitte)
> 
> ```python
> def format_as_markdown(failures):
>     """Format failures as Markdown for PR comment."""
>     if not failures:
>         return "## ✅ All Tests Passed\n\n<!-- test-failures-comment -->\nNo test failures detected."
>     
>     lines = [
>         "## ❌ Failed Tests Details",
>         "",
>         "<!-- test-failures-comment -->",
>         "",
>         f"**{len(failures)} test(s) failed**",
>         ""
>     ]
>     
>     for i, f in enumerate(failures, 1):
>         short_class = f["class"].split(".")[-1]
>         
>         lines.extend([
>             "---",
>             "",
>             f"### {i}. {short_class}.{f['test']}",
>             ""
>         ])
>         
>         # Try to parse expected/actual from message
>         parsed = parse_assertion_error(f["message"])
>         if parsed:
>             lines.extend([
>                 "**Expected:**",
>                 "```java",
>                 parsed['expected'],
>                 "```",
>                 "",
>                 "**Actual:**",
>                 "```java",
>                 parsed['actual'],
>                 "```",
>                 ""
>             ])
>         else:
>             # Fallback: show message as-is
>             lines.extend([
>                 "**Message:**",
>                 "```",
>                 f["message"],
>                 "```",
>                 ""
>             ])
>         
>         # Stack trace in collapsible section
>         lines.extend([
>             "<details>",
>             "<summary>📋 Full Stack Trace</summary>",
>             "",
>             "```",
>             f["stacktrace"],
>             "```",
>             "",
>             "</details>",
>             ""
>         ])
>     
>     return "\n".join(lines)
> ```
> 
> ### 3. Weniger aggressives Escaping
> 
> Die `escape_markdown()` Funktion wird nur noch für spezielle Fälle verwendet, nicht für den gesamten Text. Newlines bleiben erhalten.
> 
> ## Akzeptanzkriterien
> 
> - [ ] Expected/Actual werden als separate Code-Blöcke mit Java-Syntax-Highlighting angezeigt
> - [ ] Newlines bleiben erhalten für bessere Lesbarkeit
> - [ ] Jeder fehlgeschlagene Test hat seinen eigenen Abschnitt
> - [ ] Stack Traces sind in aufklappbaren Details-Abschnitten
> - [ ] Die Formatierung ist sowohl im GitHub-UI als auch für Copilot gut lesbar


</details>

title: Rewrite Tabula: Python + React (no Java)
body:
## Summary

- **Complete rewrite** of Tabula without any Java/JVM dependency
- **Backend**: Python 3.12 + FastAPI + SQLAlchemy + Alembic + PostgreSQL
- **Frontend**: React 19 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind CSS 4 + shadcn/ui
- PDF table extraction via **pdfplumber** (pure Python, replaces tabula-java)
- Client-side PDF rendering via **react-pdf** (eliminates server-side thumbnail generation)

### Backend (26 source files)
- FastAPI REST API with 21 routes covering all original functionality
- `pdfplumber` extraction with Stream / Lattice / Auto-detect modes
- `PyMuPDF` for PDF validation and metadata extraction
- Export to CSV, TSV, JSON, ZIP
- Background job processing with progress polling
- Template CRUD with import/export (.tabula-template.json compatible)

### Frontend (38 source files)
- Interactive PDF viewer with rectangular selection overlay
- Drag & drop file upload with progress tracking
- Document library, template management, extraction preview
- `navigator.clipboard` API (replaces dead Flash-based ZeroClipboard)

### Infrastructure
- Multi-stage Dockerfile (Node build + Python runtime)
- docker-compose with PostgreSQL 16
- Alembic migration for initial schema

## Test plan

- [ ] Start backend (`uvicorn app.main:app --reload`) and frontend (`npm run dev`)
- [ ] Upload a PDF with tables, verify processing job completes
- [ ] Draw selections on PDF pages, verify coordinate accuracy
- [ ] Extract tables with Stream and Lattice methods, compare results
- [ ] Export as CSV, TSV, JSON, ZIP — verify file contents
- [ ] Save/load/import/export templates
- [ ] Test with image-only PDF (should show "no text" error)
- [ ] Docker compose build and run end-to-end

feat: rewrite awake script from Jython to native Java
body:
## Summary
Converted the `awake` script from Jython (Python running on JVM) to native Java, eliminating the Jython dependency while maintaining full feature parity.

## Key Changes
- **Language migration**: Rewrote entire script from Jython to Java 11
- **Shebang update**: Changed from Jython execution to direct Java source execution using `--source 11` flag
- **Argument parsing**: Replaced argparse library with manual switch-case argument parsing
- **Time handling**: Converted from Python's `time` module to Java's `System.currentTimeMillis()` with proper millisecond-to-second conversions
- **Signal handling**: Replaced Python's signal handler with Java's `Runtime.addShutdownHook()` for graceful shutdown
- **Duration formatting**: Implemented custom `formatDuration()` method to replace Python's `timedelta` formatting
- **Class structure**: Organized code into a single `Awake` class with static methods
- **Build artifacts**: Removed `.jython_cache` from `.gitignore` as it's no longer needed

## Notable Implementation Details
- Maintains the same command-line interface with `-H`, `-M`, `-S`, and `-s` flags
- Preserves the 10-second check interval and 500ms sleep between iterations
- Uses `Point.equals()` for location comparison instead of Python's equality operator
- Properly handles time calculations in milliseconds while maintaining second-based duration logic
- Includes help text output matching the original argparse format

title: feat(sdk-java): implement java SDK
body:
This pull request introduces comprehensive support for the Java SDK across the documentation, development environment, and CI/CD pipeline. It adds Java SDK documentation, updates the devcontainer to support Java development, and ensures that Java SDK artifacts are handled in the build and publishing workflows. The changes also improve the developer experience by automating Java SDK setup and integrating Java-specific tools.

**Java SDK Documentation:**

* Added detailed Java SDK reference documentation, including `CodeInterpreter`, `ComputerUse`, and `DaytonaConfig`, to the documentation site (`apps/docs/src/content/docs/en/java-sdk/*.mdx`). [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-f630230aac9f91379cfbf9069668c0cd7d1f6cd3f0e88d8ba079b777d4c7fec9R1-R216) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-edbdbef8b77498e75f186f62c46f27f9c050012e5856e7974069d8a7c6bcd0f5R1-R374) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-2167fa629692b6934583e929376b66bc265efbd3daa18a4dc301e5cf271a437dR1-R108)
* Updated the sidebar and navigation to include the Java SDK, with new navigation categories and sidebar entries for Java SDK reference pages (`apps/docs/src/content/config.ts`). [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-788863c2d4a5f1e1f3b570408147cfd09a6488d4d1073418db7a4a4107720892R36) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-788863c2d4a5f1e1f3b570408147cfd09a6488d4d1073418db7a4a4107720892R106-R114) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-788863c2d4a5f1e1f3b570408147cfd09a6488d4d1073418db7a4a4107720892R914-R932)
* Modified the documentation header to add a link and active state for the Java SDK (`apps/docs/src/components/Header.astro`). [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-5ac6bea3de303e835de013d858a833961ece6ef3879420363ea60ccacea38cbbR32) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-5ac6bea3de303e835de013d858a833961ece6ef3879420363ea60ccacea38cbbR100-R106)

**Development Environment Enhancements:**

* Updated the devcontainer to add Java tools and features: installs Java 21, Maven, Gradle, and Ant; adds Java VS Code extensions; and configures Java project import settings (`.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`). [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-24ad71c8613ddcf6fd23818cb3bb477a1fb6d83af4550b0bad43099813088686L34-R47) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-24ad71c8613ddcf6fd23818cb3bb477a1fb6d83af4550b0bad43099813088686R75-R83)
* Adjusted the devcontainer Dockerfile to remove the installation of OpenJDK 11, as Java is now managed via devcontainer features (`.devcontainer/Dockerfile`).
* Automated Java SDK Gradle wrapper permissions and local Maven publishing in the post-start command for a smoother developer setup (`.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`).

**Build and CI/CD Pipeline Updates:**

* Added Maven-related environment variables and secrets to the SDK publishing workflow, enabling Java SDK artifact publishing (`.github/workflows/sdk_publish.yaml`).

**Repository Metadata:**

* Marked Java SDK directories as generated code in `.gitattributes` for linguist and GitHub statistics (`libs/api-client-java/**`, `libs/toolbox-api-client-java/**`).

title: Refactor: Migrate entire codebase from Java/Spring Boot to Python
body:
## Summary

Complete migration of the PreTrade multi-agent trading system from a Java/Spring Boot architecture to a pure Python implementation. All three agents (Market Analyst, Trade Executor, Learning Summary) and the Trade Dashboard web app are now implemented in Python using Flask, with shared utilities and database models refactored accordingly.

## Key Changes

**Removed (Java/Spring Boot):**
- All Maven modules: `agent-market-analyst/`, `agent-trade-executor/`, `agent-learning-summary/`, `trade-dashboard/`, `shared-db/`, `shared-utils/`
- Spring Boot application classes, controllers, services, and repositories
- JPA entity models (StockAnalysis, TradeDecision, PaperTrade, DailySummary, etc.)
- Spring configuration files (application.yml, pom.xml files)
- Java utility classes (NseClient, TimeUtils, Formatters, LotSizes)
- Comprehensive test suites across all modules
- Local setup documentation (`py/LOCAL_SETUP.md`)
- Individual agent Dockerfiles and `py/run.sh` script

**Added (Python):**
- **Shared modules** (`shared/`):
  - `nse_client.py` — HTTP client for NSE API calls with session warmup and retry logic
  - `time_utils.py` — IST time utilities for market hours and expiry calculations
  - `formatters.py` — Indian financial formatting (INR, percentages, PnL)
  - `lot_sizes.py` — NSE F&O lot size registry and validation
  - `models.py` — SQLAlchemy ORM models (replaces JPA entities)
  - `database.py` — Database connection and session management

- **Agent implementations** (Flask apps):
  - `market_analyst/app.py` — Market Analyst agent (port 8081)
    - `collectors/nse_collector.py` — NSE pre-market data, option chains, market snapshots
    - `collectors/news_collector.py` — Google News RSS and MoneyControl scraping
    - `collectors/technical_collector.py` — Volume profiles and VWAP calculations
    - `csv_export.py` — CSV export functionality
  - `trade_executor/app.py` — Trade Executor agent (port 8082)
  - `trade_dashboard/app.py` — Trade Dashboard web app (port 8080)
    - `csv_parser.py` — CSV signal parsing
    - `static/css/dashboard.css` — Dashboard styling
    - `templates/` — HTML templates (dashboard, upload, base)
  - `learning_summary/app.py` — Learning Summary agent (port 8083)

- **Test suite** (`tests/`):
  - `test_lot_sizes.py`, `test_time_utils.py`, `test_formatters.py`
  - `test_csv_parser.py`, `test_technical_collector.py`

- **Docker & Build**:
  - Individual Dockerfiles for each agent (`Dockerfile.analyst`, `Dockerfile.dashboard`, `Dockerfile.executor`, `Dockerfile.learner`)
  - Updated `docker-compose.yml` to reference new Dockerfiles
  - Simplified `scripts/build.sh` and `scripts/run.sh` for Python workflow
  - Updated `scripts/test.sh` for pytest

**Modified:**
- `requirements.txt` — Added pytest dependency
- `.gitignore` — Removed Maven-specific patterns, kept Python patterns
- `CHANGELOG.md` — Updated to reflect Python-based architecture
- `CLAUDE.md` — Updated system description
- `COMMIT_LOG.md` — Added migration commit entry
- `docker-compose.yml` — Updated service build contexts

## Implementation Details

- **Database**: Migrated from JPA/Hibernate to SQLAlchemy ORM with same schema
- **API Clients**: Replicated NSE client with exponential backoff retry logic and session warmup
- **Time Handling**: All IST operations centralized in `shared/time_utils.py` (no direct `datetime.now()` calls)
- **Configuration**: Environment variables replace

title: feat: v0.3.0
body:
## v0.3.0 — Grimoire

This branch takes cantrip from "spec-generated code that passes conformance tests" to "framework that works with real LLMs." It adds the Grimoire appendix to the spec, rewrites all examples as teaching programs, and fixes every framework bug that surfaced when running against gpt-5-mini and claude-sonnet.

69 files changed, +5250 / −2177 across py/clj/ex.

---

## What changed and why

### 1. Spec: Appendix A — Grimoire (5d354e6, 26c5f1b)

Twelve patterns (A.1–A.12) showing what you build with the spec's vocabulary, from a raw LLM query through the familiar. Synthesized from six drafts across three models (Claude, Codex, Gemini). Informative, not normative — no new conformance rules beyond chapters 1–7.

The spec itself was tightened from 17,062 → 11,528 words (−32%), all 77 behavioral rules preserved. Redundant prose cut, sacred passages (entity emergence, lethal trifecta, ghost library) kept at full strength. Version bumped 0.2.0 → 0.3.0.

### 2. Teaching examples replace conformance theater (a9884bf → afbda85)

The old examples were conformance theater — opaque helpers hiding construction, toy arithmetic intents, FakeLLM-only. The new examples are teaching programs:

- **Visible construction**: every Cantrip/Circle/Identity built inline with named fields
- **Realistic intents**: SaaS metrics analysis, Q3 revenue drivers, churn risk scoring
- **Spec vocabulary**: comments reference rule IDs (CIRCLE-1, WARD-1, COMP-7)
- **Narrative output**: `println`/`print()` tells the story as the example runs
- **Non-happy paths**: circle rejection, error steering, truncation, ward enforcement
- **Dual mode**: FakeLLM for CI, real LLM for demos (env vars control which)

Python: 12 examples. Clojure: 13 (extra ACP pattern). Elixir: 12.

### 3. Framework fixes — making real LLMs work

Running examples against real LLMs exposed bugs in all three implementations. These are the substantive framework changes, not just example tweaks.

#### Python (d111473, b70d4fd, d16c60e)
- **InProcessPythonExecutor**: real Python via `exec()` with gate functions injected, replaces MiniCodeExecutor (regex toy) as the default code medium executor
- **done gate schema**: `_DONE_PARAMETERS` gives done an `answer` param so LLMs know what to pass
- **tool_choice="required"**: when `require_done_tool=True`, forces LLM to use tools
- **Child identity**: generic prompt instead of inheriting parent's delegation instructions (which reference gates unavailable at child depth)
- **Subtractive builtins ward**: start with full builtins, subtract dangerous ones — matches spec's ward model
- **call_gate exposed**: code medium namespace now includes `call_gate()` matching capability_text
- **system_prompt in call_entity**: parent can set child identity via request
- **context in call_entity**: parent can pass context data, prepended to child intent

#### Clojure (4bfad34, e1a169b, b1670dd, d01a8f4, 6d23bb7)
- **clojure.data.json**: replaces 170-line hand-rolled JSON parser. The old parser was a liability — any escape handling edge case silently corrupted LLM code output going into SCI
- **max_completion_tokens: 16384**: prevents OpenAI API from silently truncating tool call arguments. Uses `max_completion_tokens` (not `max_tokens`) for gpt-5-mini compatibility
- **done gate answer schema**: LLMs know to pass `{answer: "..."}` not bare strings
- **String key support in done-observation**: real LLM JSON has string keys, not keywords
- **tool-call-id propagation**: observations carry IDs through the full pipeline
- **tool_choice :required**: auto-set when require-done-tool is true
- **Code medium tool-call synthesis**: wraps raw content responses in `clojure` tool-call format for OpenAI message threading
- **Gate schemas always include "properties"**: OpenAI requires it even for empty schemas
- **FakeLLM skips tool_choice enforcement**: scripted tests shouldn't fail on tool_choice validation
- **Capability text for code medium**: SCI sandbox physics + host function docs injected into LLM context. Correctly documents fire-and-forget semantics (call-gate/submit-answer emit, call-agent blocks and returns)
- **SCI capability text improvements**: explicit Java interop prohibition, no defprotocol/defrecord/deftype, synchronous call-agent semantics
- **Generic child system prompt**: children get "You are a child entity" instead of inheriting the parent's coordinator prompt that tells them to delegate recursively
- **Strip delegation gates from children**: `derive-child-cantrip` removes `call_entity`/`call_entity_batch` gates, preventing runaway recursion (matches TS reference)
- **Cap child max-turns at 3**: bounds error cascade duration when children write invalid SCI code
- **context and system_prompt in call-agent**: parent can pass context and override child identity
- **LLM timeout bumped to 120s**: code medium with real LLMs needs more time
- **dotenv .env loading**: examples load API keys from `.env` file

#### Elixir (4bfad34, 94b2457, b1670dd, d01a8f4, 6d23bb7)
- **done gate answer schema**: matching Clojure fix
- **put_in on Cantrip struct**: `put_in` doesn't work on Elixir structs (no Access protocol). Changed to `%{struct | field: value}` syntax. This was silently breaking ALL `call_entity` calls with `system_prompt`
- **Generic child system prompt**: same fix as Clojure — children don't inherit coordinator identity
- **defmodule prohibition in capability_presentation**: prevents LLM from writing `defmodule` in code medium
- **context and system_prompt in call_entity**: matching Python/Clojure
- **LLM timeout bumped to 120s**: matching Clojure
- **Example 12 rewrite**: uses `call_entity.()` binding instead of manual `Cantrip.new/1` construction (LLM can't know the LLM config tuple)

### 4. Prompt synthesis parity (e1a169b)

Code medium now injects sandbox physics and host function documentation into the LLM context across all implementations, matching TS's `capabilityDocs()` pattern:

- **Python**: `CodeMedium.capability_text()` → runtime injects as first system message
- **Clojure**: `capability-text` + `tool-view` → `build-messages` injects in runtime
- **Elixir**: `capability_presentation` (already had this)

Key insight: Clojure code medium is fire-and-forget (`call-gate` emits, doesn't return inline). Python code medium is synchronous (`call_gate` executes and returns). `call-agent` is synchronous in both. Capability text must reflect these differences per language.

---

## How it was built

Wave 1: Write grimoire examples for all three languages using parallel Codex agents with dense prompts (SPEC, TS reference examples, API surface docs, red-green workflow). All three produced passing code on first try.

Wave 2: Review agents found structural problems — Elixir recreated conformance theater with new names, Python used private APIs, Clojure had FakeLLM-only examples. Fix agents resolved: Elixir full rewrite, Python/Clojure targeted fixes.

Wave 3: Real LLM verification exposed framework bugs. Each bug was fixed, verified, and the next example tested. Python converged fastest (organic implementation). Clojure needed the most framework work (SCI sandbox + hand-rolled JSON). Elixir had one critical bug (`put_in` on structs).

Wave 4: Deep debugging of Clojure example 12 (familiar). Used stderr tracing in the runtime loop to observe turn-by-turn behavior. Found: children inherited coordinator prompt → tried to delegate recursively. Children inherited code medium → gpt-5-mini writes invalid SCI Clojure (Math/round, malformed parens, duplicate case constants). Fixed with generic child identity, delegation gate stripping, max-turns cap, proper JSON parser, and max_completion_tokens.

---

## Test results

| Language | Tests | Status |
|----------|-------|--------|
| Python | 227 passed, 2 skipped | All green |
| Clojure | 110 tests, 261 assertions | All green |
| Elixir | 170 tests, 0 failures | All green |
| TypeScript | 615 pass, 55 skip | Unchanged (reference impl) |

### Real LLM verification
- **Python**: all 12 examples verified with real LLM
- **Clojure**: examples 01–11 verified; example 12 completes but slow (~5–10 min)
- **Elixir**: examples 01, 04, 12 verified with gpt-5-mini

---

## Known limitations

**Clojure example 12 (familiar) is slow with real LLM.** gpt-5-mini consistently writes Java interop (`Math/round`, `Math/exp`) in SCI sandbox code despite capability text prohibiting it. Children error-steer through all turns before truncating. The framework handles this correctly (error observation → retry → truncation → parent sees failure) — it's a model capability issue, not a framework bug. A stronger Clojure-aware model or conversation-medium children would resolve it.

**Ghost implementations caveat.** py/clj/ex are spec-generated, not battle-tested like TS. This branch significantly closes the gap but the TS implementation remains the reference for edge cases.

title: Rewrite COMA algorithm in pure Python
body:
## Summary

- **Adds `ComaPy`**, a pure Python implementation of the COMA 3.0 schema matching algorithm, eliminating the Java 6 runtime dependency (40MB JAR + subprocess IPC)
- Faithfully reproduces COMA's matching pipeline: trigram (Dice) name/path similarity, TF-IDF instance matching with global IDF (matching Lucene 3.x `sqrt(tf) * log(N/df)`), and structural matchers (leaves, parents) — all combined via max-matching Dice set aggregation
- Supports `use_schema`, `use_instances`, `delta`, `threshold`, and `max_n` parameters for full control over matching behavior
- **Deprecates the Java `Coma` class** with a `DeprecationWarning`; `ComaPy` will be renamed to `Coma` in valentine v1.0.0
- Adds 68 tests: regression tests on two datasets (easy + hard with different column names/abbreviations) and unit tests for all internal modules (combination, selection, trigram, TF-IDF, schema graph, datatype similarity)
- Updates README with `ComaPy` documentation and deprecation notice for Java `Coma`

body:
## Summary
- **Language-agnostic agent chart (#580)**: Remove Python bias from `mcp-mesh-agent` Helm chart. Add `agent.runtime` field and `isPython` helper for conditional Python env var injection. Remove dead `agent.script` and `agent.python` fields. Rewrite README with Python/TypeScript/Java examples.
- **Arbitrary namespace support (#579)**: Replace hardcoded FQDN hostnames with short names in `mcp-mesh-core` values. Add `| default .Release.Namespace` fallback to namespace template. Add `networkPolicy.allowedNamespace` for dynamic ingress rules. Document custom namespace, multi-tenant, and cross-namespace patterns.
- **ENTRYPOINT/CMD alignment**: Fix TypeScript runtime ENTRYPOINT (`node` → `npx tsx`). Align all scaffold Dockerfiles so CMD only provides the script/jar path (ENTRYPOINT provides the runtime command). Update Go handlers to match.
- **NetworkPolicy fix**: AND namespaceSelector with podSelector in registry ingress (was incorrectly OR'd as separate `from` entries).
- **Scaffold improvements**: Add `agent.runtime` to all helm-values templates. Add Docker/K8s/Documentation sections to Java/Python basic READMEs. Remove obsolete "Adding Tools" sections. Add cross-namespace comments to registryUrl.

## Review Notes
- Short service names replace FQDNs in mcp-mesh-core defaults — intentional for namespace flexibility, but a breaking change for cross-namespace users (documented override path)
- Duplicate PYTHONUNBUFFERED in ConfigMap + deployment env (minor, explicit env wins)

Closes #579
Closes #580

## Test plan
- [x] `helm lint` passes for all 3 charts (agent, core, registry)
- [x] `helm template` Python agent includes PYTHONUNBUFFERED/PYTHONPATH/UVICORN_* env vars
- [x] `helm template` TypeScript/Java agent does NOT include Python env vars
- [x] `helm template` with `--set agent.runtime=python` injects Python env vars regardless of image name
- [x] Registry NetworkPolicy renders AND'd selectors (namespace + podSelector in same from entry)
- [x] `go build` and `go test` pass for handlers
- [ ] Deploy Python/TypeScript/Java agents to K8s cluster and verify registration

refactor(ci): Enhance cicddiagnostics skill Investigation flexib (#33760)
body:
## Description

Refactored cicd-diagnostics skill from bash to Python for improved compatibility, maintainability, and Claude Code skill standards compliance.

## Motivation for Python Migration

The original bash implementation had several critical issues that necessitated the migration to Python:

### 1. Non-standard for Claude Code Skills
- Python is the **recommended and standard language** for Claude Code skills
- Better integration with Claude Code's error handling and output formatting
- Structured output and parameter parsing that Claude can reliably interpret

### 2. Shell Compatibility Problems
- **Bash-specific features** don't work across all shell environments (bash vs zsh vs sh)
- Array handling differs between shells, causing failures on different systems
- String manipulation and advanced features have varying support
- Portability issues across macOS (zsh default), Linux (bash), and CI environments

### 3. Limited Error Handling
- Bash error handling is primitive (set -e, exit codes)
- Difficult to provide meaningful error messages to Claude
- Hard to implement graceful failure recovery
- Debugging bash scripts is significantly more difficult

### 4. Parameter Validation Complexity
- Validating complex parameters in bash is verbose and error-prone
- Type checking is difficult (everything is a string)
- Parsing structured data (JSON, arrays) requires external tools (jq, etc.)

### 5. External Service Detection Requirements
- Need robust HTTP libraries for GitHub API status checks
- JSON parsing for dependency health checks
- Better error handling for network failures

## Benefits of Python Implementation

- **Standard library richness**: Built-in JSON, HTTP, regex, data structures
- **Superior error handling**: Try/except with meaningful exceptions
- **Type hints**: Clear parameter types and validation
- **Maintainability**: More readable, easier to extend
- **Cross-platform**: Consistent behavior across environments
- **Testing**: Better unit testing capabilities

## Changes

- Converted all bash diagnostic scripts to Python modules
- Added parameter validation with clear error messages
- Implemented external issue detection (GitHub API status, dependency checks)
- Enhanced investigation workflow with decision-tree approach
- Improved documentation with Python examples
- Added comprehensive error handling and logging

## Testing

- Tested investigation workflows with various failure types
- Validated parameter parsing and error handling
- Verified external issue detection capabilities
- Confirmed compatibility across different environments

Closes #33760

This PR fixes: #33760

title: feat(java-core): add java-clean-arch skill
body:
Covers Clean Architecture / Hexagonal (Ports & Adapters) and DDD:
- Review mode: detects dependency rule violations (domain importing Spring/JPA, business logic in controllers, anemic domain model) and DDD tactical pattern opportunities (value objects, rich entities)
- Implement mode: scaffolds full hexagonal package layout with domain/application/infrastructure separation
- DDD mode: value objects (records for Java 16+), rich domain entities with domain events, port interfaces, persistence adapters

Phased migration path from layered to hexagonal architecture (5 phases, each independently testable).

references/patterns.md contains complete templates for use cases, application services, REST adapters, JPA entities, domain events, and ArchUnit tests to enforce dependency rules.

title: feat(java-spring): add java-resilience skill
body:
Covers Resilience4J for Spring Boot 2.x and 3.x:
- review mode: audits existing config for missing thresholds, wrong retry/circuit-breaker stacking, retrying business errors, no actuator
- circuit-breaker mode: COUNT_BASED sliding window, slow-call threshold, state transition logging via CircuitBreakerOnStateTransitionEvent
- retry mode: exponential backoff, ignore-exceptions for 4xx/business errors, result predicate for HTTP status codes
- rate-limiter mode: per-period limits with permit timeout
- bulkhead mode: semaphore vs thread-pool bulkhead choice guide
- timeout mode: @TimeLimiter for async (CompletableFuture/Flux)

references/patterns.md has full application.yml + Java code for all modes, annotation stacking order, and Actuator endpoint reference.

title: feat(java-spring): add java-cache skill
body:

- review mode: checks @EnableCaching presence, unbounded caches, missing eviction, JDK serialization in Redis, null caching, proxy bypass pitfalls
- setup mode: Caffeine (single-instance) vs Redis (distributed) guidance
- cacheable/evict/redis modes with full code examples
- references/patterns.md: per-cache TTL config, @Cacheable/@CacheEvict/ @CachePut/@Caching patterns, conditional caching, Redis cluster/sentinel config, Actuator metrics endpoints, common pitfalls table

title: Bump com.graphql-java:graphql-java from 20.9 to 25.0
body:
Bumps [com.graphql-java:graphql-java](https://github.com/graphql-java/graphql-java) from 20.9 to 25.0.
Release notes

Sourced from com.graphql-java:graphql-java's releases.

25.0

Key changes

Dataloader

Refactoring strategy

The existing PerLevelDataLoaderStrategy has been refactored which lead to simplifications and performance improvements.

New strategies

Two new strategies were introduced: CHAINED and EXHAUSTED. Both can be configured via UnusalConfiguration (see next section).

CHAINED allow for chained DataLoaders to be used while keeping a per level dispatch strategies.

EXHAUSTED is a completely new strategy that works on the basis to dispatch once the engine is not busy. It mirrors the JS data loader dispatch strategy, but for a multi threaded system.

Unusual Configuration

A more generalised configuration mechanism has been added for "unusual configuration". By that we mean configuation we dont expect many people to use but if they do its now in a more common place

For example if you wanted to change the maximum depth the document parser will accept you could call the following methods.

        var parserOptions = newParserOptions().maxRuleDepth(99).build()
        GraphQL.unusualConfiguration().parsing().setDefaultParserOptions(parserOptions)

JSpecify Annotations

The team are starting to embrace https://jspecify.dev/ annotations as the way to indicate nullable and non nullable fields. Many important classes have had these annotations added to help make it more semantically clear when a value can be null or not.

Breaking Changes

A wrapping FetchedValue object is not always returned on field fetchers for performance reasons. This means that graphql.execution.instrumentation.parameters.InstrumentationFieldCompleteParameters#getFetchedObject was created to replace the older getFetchedValue method and the returns object can sometimes be a FetchedValue or sometimes a simple POJO value.

Performance improvements

A series of performance improvements have been made to reduce the memory footprint of the library. Also the Java .stream() operator can be slower than a more direct loop and many of these calls have been changed for performance reasons.

  • avoid wrapping materialized fieldValueObject in a CompletableFuture by @​samuelAndalon in graphql-java/graphql-java#3943
  • ExecutionStrategyParameters now has a direct transform without a Builder by @​bbakerman in graphql-java/graphql-java#3935
  • ExecutionStepInfo now has a direct transform without a Builder by @​bbakerman in graphql-java/graphql-java#3934
  • FpKit now longer uses streams for performance reasons by @​bbakerman in graphql-java/graphql-java#3932
  • Implement toString/hashCode/equals for DataFetcherResult by @​AlexandreCarlton in graphql-java/graphql-java#3964

title: fix(python): emit Pydantic field aliases to fix nested field validation
body:
## Description
Linear ticket: [FER-8077](https://linear.app/buildwithfern/issue/FER-8077/elevenlabs-pydantic-v2-decoding-error-for-nested-field) 
<!-- Use "Closes" to automatically close the ticket when PR merges, or "Refs" to link without closing -->
<!-- Provide a clear and concise description of the changes made in this PR -->

A previous fix, #11011 only helped when callers invoked `model_validate()` directly, but it did not apply to nested validation paths (e.g. when a parent model is validated via `TypeAdapter`), so wire keys like `is_base64_encoded` could still fail with “Field required” inside nested objects. With this PR we will now emit real `pydantic.Field(alias=...)` for aliased fields and update core parsing utilities so nested/union validation works reliably across Pydantic v1 and v2. The fix preserves Python attribute names and adds safe handling for Python-field-name inputs with collision protection.

## Changes Made
<!-- List the main changes and updates implemented in this PR -->
- Aliased fields are now generated as `pydantic.Field(alias="<wire_name>")`, ensuring Pydantic validates wire keys correctly (including nested `TypeAdapter` cases).
- `parse_obj_as` now conditionally pre-dealiases only when needed (TypedDict / FieldMetadata-only paths), avoiding regressions with models that have native Pydantic aliases.
- Added a pre-validation rewrite from python field names to aliases with explicit collision detection
- Removed obsolete override: dropped the Pydantic v2 `model_validate` override in `UncheckedBaseModel` since aliasing is handled natively.
 
## Testing
<!-- Describe how you tested these changes -->
- [x] Unit tests added/updated
- [x] Manual testing completed


<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Ensures wire-key validation works reliably (including nested `TypeAdapter`/unions) by moving to native Pydantic aliases and tightening core parsing.
> 
> - Generate aliased fields with `pydantic.Field(alias="...")`; update codegen to always set alias when a field is aliased
> - Update `parse_obj_as` to only pre-dealias when required (e.g., TypedDict or FieldMetadata-only), preserving native alias handling
> - Add pre-validation rewrite from Python field names to aliases with explicit collision detection in `UniversalBaseModel` (v1/v2)
> - Remove the Pydantic v2 `model_validate` override from `UncheckedBaseModel`
> - Add hardened v1-on-v2 core utilities (checks against `pydantic.v1.BaseModel`) and a regression test
> - Regenerate seed SDKs to reflect new aliasing and utilities; incidental lockfile bumps
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 0a75f340cb488a692a3db75a53106b3c8739ca9b. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

title: fix(typescript, java, go, csharp): check error count in findMatchingUndiscriminatedUnionType for TS, Java, Go, C#
body: ## Description Refs: Customer issue where `options` (a `oneOf` field modeled as an undiscriminated union) renders as `{}` in generated code snippets for TypeScript, Java, Go, and C#. Ports the Python dynamic snippets fix to all other languages. The Python generator already had the correct behavior after v0.0.5, but the other language generators were still broken. ## Changes Made The root cause is in `findMatchingUndiscriminatedUnionType` across all non-Python dynamic snippet packages. When iterating union variants to find a match, the code only checked whether the conversion returned `nop`. However, a variant can match *structurally* (e.g., an object with all-optional fields converts to a non-nop empty object `{}`) while still being the **wrong** variant — indicated by errors added during conversion (e.g., for missing required fields on the correct variant). The fix adds `this.context.errors.size() > errorsBefore` as a rejection condition alongside the existing `isNop` check, matching the Python generator's pattern: - **TypeScript** (`typescript-v2/dynamic-snippets`): Added error count check to existing `isNop` condition - **Go** (`go-v2/dynamic-snippets`): Added `errorsBefore` tracking, error count check, and `truncate` calls - **C#** (`csharp/dynamic-snippets`): Added `errorsBefore` tracking, `is.Literal.nop()` check (previously missing entirely — was accepting the first variant unconditionally), error count check, and `truncate` calls - **Java** (`java-v2/dynamic-snippets`): Added error count check; simplified by removing verbose error logging/throwing workaround that was masking the real issue; removed unused `extractErrorMessage` import Updated snapshot tests for **C#** (2 snapshots) and **Go** (3 snapshots) — these previously captured error arrays (e.g., `"Expected number, got boolean"`) for mixed-type union maps and now correctly produce valid code snippets with all union variants resolved. ### Version bumps Added `versions.yml` changelog entries (patch, `fix`) for all four generators: | Generator | Version | |-----------|---------| | TypeScript | 3.63.3 | | Java | 4.1.3 | | Go | 1.34.3 | | C# | 2.59.1 | ## Human review checklist 1. **Java behavior change**: Previously threw an error when no variant matched; now returns `undefined` (consistent with all other languages). The caller already handles `undefined` → `nop()`. Verify this doesn't break any downstream consumers. 2. **C# was the most broken**: It had no `nop` check or error tracking at all — it unconditionally returned the first variant's conversion result. This is the largest behavioral change. 3. **Java error message simplified**: The detailed variant-by-variant error logging (`attemptedVariants`, `variantErrors`) was removed in favor of the simpler message used by all other languages. This loses some diagnostic detail but aligns the implementations. 4. **irVersion values**: TS/Go/C# use irVersion 66 (matching their latest entries), Java uses 65 (matching its latest). Verify these are correct. ## Testing - [x] Snapshot tests updated (C# and Go `map-prim-to-union` mixed values) - [x] All snapshot tests pass locally (C# 34/34, Go 96/96, Java 32/32, TS 32/32) - [x] Lint/typecheck passes (`pnpm run check`) - [ ] Manual testing with auth0-myorg-snippets to confirm `options` field renders correctly Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/d5cbcb909c0d4af685b1ed626b81cb1d Requested by: @patrickthornton ---

title: Rewrite tooling in Java and drop Python scripts
body:
### Motivation
- Replace the existing Python-based tooling with Java implementations so the project is maintained in Java as requested (`refais tout en java / supprime le python`).
- Provide CLI programs that work with standard Java tooling and remove the Python dependency footprint.

### Description
- Added two Java CLI programs: `com.ratemywine.CollectRatings` (CSV-driven DuckDuckGo search + regex extraction) and `com.ratemywine.MilleniumWineRatingsScraper` (site crawler extracting JSON-LD and text ratings) under `src/main/java/com/ratemywine/`.
- Updated `README.md` with Java 17+ compilation (`javac -d out src/main/java/com/ratemywine/*.java`) and usage instructions for both tools.
- Removed the legacy Python scripts `millenium_wine_ratings_scraper.py` and `scripts/collect_ratings.py` and updated `requirements.txt` to indicate Python is no longer used.
- Added simple CLI parsing helpers and CSV/JSON output routines in the Java implementations to match previous output formats.

### Testing
- Compiled the Java sources with `javac -d out src/main/java/com/ratemywine/*.java` which succeeded.
- Ran the crawler with `java -cp out com.ratemywine.MilleniumWineRatingsScraper https://example.com --max-pages 1 --min-delay 0 --max-delay 0 --timeout 5 --csv /tmp/wine.csv --json /tmp/wine.json` and the program executed but failed HTTP requests due to environment network restrictions (`Network is unreachable`).
- Ran the CSV-driven flow with a sample file via `java -cp out com.ratemywine.CollectRatings --input /tmp/wines.csv --output /tmp/ratings.csv --max-results 1 --timeout 3 --sleep 0` which produced a valid output CSV containing a `no-candidate-page` status (expected given no network search results).

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69a0bccd203883219fb6778344d7a9b1)

title: fix: Java cookbook recipes to compile with copilot-sdk-java 0.2.1-java.1
body:
## Summary

Fix compilation errors and documentation inaccuracies in Java cookbook recipes against the `copilot-sdk-java:0.2.1-java.1` API.

## Issues Found & Fixed

### Compilation failures (recipe/*.java)

| Recipe | Issue | Fix |
|--------|-------|-----|
| `MultipleSessions.java` | `CopilotSession.destroy()` does not exist | Replaced with `close()` |
| `AccessibilityReport.java` | `McpServerConfig` class does not exist | Replaced with `Map.of()` (matches `setMcpServers(Map<String, Object>)` signature) |

### Documentation inaccuracies (*.md inline code)

| File | Issue | Fix |
|------|-------|-----|
| `multiple-sessions.md` | `destroy()` in 2 code snippets | Replaced with `close()` |
| `accessibility-report.md` | `McpServerConfig` in 2 code snippets | Replaced with `Map.of()` |
| `error-handling.md` | `session.addTool()`, `ToolDefinition.builder()`, `ToolResultObject.success/error` — none exist | Replaced with `ToolDefinition.create()`, `SessionConfig.setTools()`, `CompletableFuture.completedFuture()` |

## Verification

All 7 recipes compile successfully with `jbang build`:

```
✅ ErrorHandling.java OK
✅ MultipleSessions.java OK
✅ ManagingLocalFiles.java OK
✅ PersistingSessions.java OK
✅ PRVisualization.java OK
✅ RalphLoop.java OK
✅ AccessibilityReport.java OK
```

## No changes needed

These recipes were already correct:
- `ErrorHandling.java` / `error-handling.md` (runnable file was fine; only docs section fixed)
- `ManagingLocalFiles.java` / `managing-local-files.md`
- `PersistingSessions.java` / `persisting-sessions.md`
- `PRVisualization.java` / `pr-visualization.md`
- `RalphLoop.java` / `ralph-loop.md`

title: Fix safe-inputs `script:` runner always returning `{"stdout":"","stderr":""}`
body:
- [x] Understand the bug: `generateSafeInputJavaScriptToolScript` generates a `.cjs` that only exports `execute()` but never calls it when run as subprocess
- [x] Fix `generateSafeInputJavaScriptToolScript` in `pkg/workflow/safe_inputs_generator.go` to add a `require.main === module` block that reads stdin, calls `execute()`, and writes JSON to stdout
- [x] Refactor main execution block to use async/await instead of Promise chaining
- [x] Move the main execution harness to `actions/setup/js/safe-inputs-runner.cjs`; generated scripts delegate via `require('./safe-inputs-runner.cjs')(execute)`
- [x] Add `actions/setup/js/safe-inputs-runner.test.cjs` with 4 Vitest tests (happy path, empty stdin, JSON parse error, execute error)
- [x] Update `actions/setup/setup.sh` to copy `safe-inputs-runner.cjs` to the safe-inputs directory
- [x] Update test in `safe_inputs_generator_test.go` to assert `require('./safe-inputs-runner.cjs')` delegation
- [x] Fix TS18046: `catch (e)` variable is `unknown`; use `e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)` guard
- [x] All tests pass: `make fmt`, TypeScript check, Vitest tests

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Bug: `safe-inputs` with `script:` always returns `{"stdout":"","stderr":""}`</issue_title>
> <issue_description># Bug: `safe-inputs` with `script:` always returns `{"stdout":"","stderr":""}`
> 
> ## Summary
> 
> A `safe-inputs` entry using the `script:` runner always produces `{"stdout":"","stderr":""}` when called, regardless of what the script does. The return value is never surfaced to the agent.
> 
> ## Steps to Reproduce
> 
> Define a `safe-input` with `script:`:
> 
> ```yaml
> safe-inputs:
>   github-graphql:
>     inputs:
>       query:
>         type: string
>         required: true
>       variables:
>         type: string
>         required: false
>     env:
>       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
>     script: |
>       const graphql = require('@octokit/graphql').graphql.defaults({
>         headers: { authorization: `token ${process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN}` }
>       });
>       const vars = inputs.variables ? JSON.parse(inputs.variables) : {};
>       const result = await graphql(inputs.query, vars);
>       return result;
> ```
> 
> Call it from a workflow. The agent receives:
> 
> ```
> {"stdout":"","stderr":""}
> ```
> 
> ## What I Tried
> 
> - Using `return result` (object)
> - Using `console.log(JSON.stringify(result))` — no output in stdout
> - Simplified scripts (e.g. `return { viewer: { login: "test" } }`) — still empty
> - The same logic works fine with `py:` using `print(json.dumps(...))` — the Python runner correctly captures stdout
> 
> ## Expected Behavior
> 
> The `script:` runner should:
> 1. Capture the `return` value and serialize it as the tool output as documented
> 
> ## Actual Behavior
> 
> Every `script:` safe-input returns `{"stdout":"","stderr":""}` unconditionally. The agent sees no data and has to assume defaults / skip logic that depended on the result.
> 
> ## Impact
> 
> Workflows that rely on `script:` for GitHub GraphQL queries (or any JS-based safe-input) silently fail. The only workaround is to rewrite the logic in Python using `py:` or use `run:` with shell commands, which is significantly less ergonomic for GraphQL/JSON workloads.
> 
> ## Environment
> 
> - `gh aw` (github/gh-aw) — current version in use
> - Runner: GitHub Actions (AWF sandbox)
> </issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

  - Lock FFT size and bytes per sample at capture start to prevent
    buffer misalignment when user changes UI mid-capture                                                                                                                                                                    
  - Pre-allocate audio buffers once instead of per-frame allocation

## Latest Change

Updated the ES module main check pattern in all scripts to use `import.meta.url.endsWith(process.argv[1])` instead of `import.meta.url === \`file://${process.argv[1]}\``. This provides better cross-platform compatibility between Windows and Unix path formats.

All review comments have been addressed. The codebase now properly uses ES modules throughout.

title: Rewrite backend from Java Spring Boot + PostgreSQL to Python FastAPI + MongoDB
body:
Complete backend rewrite from Java/Spring Boot/PostgreSQL to Python/FastAPI/MongoDB while maintaining API contract compatibility.

## Architecture Changes

- **Framework**: Spring Boot 3.2 → FastAPI 0.104
- **Database**: PostgreSQL (relational) → MongoDB 7.0 (document store)
- **Driver**: JPA/Hibernate → Motor (async MongoDB driver)
- **Runtime**: Synchronous Java → Native async/await Python

## Implementation

### Core Application
- `app/main.py`: FastAPI app with lifespan management for DB connections
- `app/config/database.py`: Motor async client with credential masking in logs
- CORS configured for ports 5173, 3000 (Vite/React dev servers)

### Data Models
- `app/models/todo.py`: Pydantic v2 models with custom ObjectId handler
- TodoCreate/TodoUpdate/TodoResponse schemas with validation (title 1-200 chars, description max 1000)
- Proper `__get_pydantic_core_schema__` implementation (v2 compatible, not deprecated `__get_validators__`)

### API Endpoints
All REST endpoints preserved at `/api/todos`:
- `GET /api/todos?completed={bool}` - List with optional filter
- `GET /api/todos/{id}` - Retrieve by ObjectId
- `GET /api/todos/search?title={query}` - Case-insensitive regex search
- `POST /api/todos` - Create with auto timestamps
- `PUT /api/todos/{id}` - Partial update with timestamp refresh
- `DELETE /api/todos/{id}` - Single delete
- `DELETE /api/todos` - Bulk delete (dev/test only, warning added)

### Deployment
- Dockerfile: Multi-stage Python 3.11 slim image
- docker-compose.yml: MongoDB 7.0 + backend services
- fly.toml: Fly.io deployment config with health checks
- start-backend.sh: Dev setup with venv management

## Code Reduction
- Java backend: ~1000+ LOC with boilerplate
- Python backend: 380 LOC total
- Auto-generated OpenAPI docs replace manual Swagger config

## Example Usage

```python
# Async route handler with MongoDB
@router.post("", response_model=TodoResponse, status_code=201)
async def create_todo(todo: TodoCreate):
    db = await get_database()
    now = datetime.utcnow()
    todo_dict = {**todo.model_dump(), "createdAt": now, "updatedAt": now}
    result = await db.todos.insert_one(todo_dict)
    return todo_helper(await db.todos.find_one({"_id": result.inserted_id}))
```

## Security
- Credentials masked in connection logs
- Input validation via Pydantic constraints
- Proper HTTP status codes (400/404/422)
- CodeQL scan: 0 vulnerabilities

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->



<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

# 📝 TodoList Application

A full-stack TodoList application built with **Vite + React** (frontend), **Java Spring Boot** (backend), and **PostgreSQL** (database) for OOP and Web & Internet courses.

## 🚀 Features

- ✅ Create, Read, Update, and Delete todos (CRUD operations)
- ✅ Mark todos as complete/incomplete
- ✅ Edit todo title and description
- ✅ Filter todos (All, Active, Completed)
- ✅ Responsive and modern UI
- ✅ RESTful API architecture
- ✅ PostgreSQL database integration
- ✅ JPA/Hibernate for ORM

## 🏗️ Project Structure

```
todolist-app/
├── backend/                    # Spring Boot backend
│   ├── src/
│   │   └── main/
│   │       ├── java/com/todolist/backend/
│   │       │   ├── controller/    # REST controllers
│   │       │   ├── model/         # Entity models
│   │       │   ├── repository/    # JPA repositories
│   │       │   └── service/       # Business logic
│   │       └── resources/
│   │           └── application.properties
│   └── build.gradle           # Gradle dependencies
│
└── frontend/                  # Vite + React frontend
    ├── src/
    │   ├── components/        # React components
    │   ├── services/          # API services
    │   ├── App.jsx            # Main app component
    │   └── main.jsx           # Entry point
    └── package.json           # NPM dependencies
```

## 📋 Prerequisites

Before running this application, make sure you have the following installed:

- **Java 17** or higher
- **Gradle 8.5+** (or use the included Gradle wrapper)
- **Node.js 18+** and **npm**
- **PostgreSQL 14+** (or Docker to run PostgreSQL in a container)

## 🗄️ Database Setup

### Option 1: Using Docker (Recommended)

The easiest way to run PostgreSQL is using Docker:

```bash
docker-compose up -d
```

This will start PostgreSQL on port 5432 with the database `todolist_db` already created.

### Option 2: Manual PostgreSQL Installation

1. Install and start PostgreSQL server

2. Create the database:
   ```sql
   CREATE DATABASE todolist_db;
   CREATE USER todouser WITH PASSWORD 'password';
   GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE todolist_db TO todouser;
   ```

3. Update database credentials in `backend/src/main/resources/application.properties`:
   ```properties
   spring.datasource.url=jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/todolist_db
   spring.datasource.username=todouser
   spring.datasource.password=your_password
   ```

## 🚀 Running the Application

### Quick Start

For the easiest setup experience, use the provided shell scripts:

1. **Start the database** (if using Docker):
   ```bash
   docker-compose up -d
   ```

2. **Start the backend** (in one terminal):
   ```bash
   ./start-backend.sh
   ```

3. **Start the frontend** (in another terminal):
   ```bash
   ./start-frontend.sh
   ```

4. Open your browser at `http://localhost:5173`

### Manual Setup

#### Backend (Spring Boot)

1. Navigate to the backend directory:
   ```bash
   cd backend
   ```

2. Build and run the application:
   ```bash
   ./gradlew bootRun
   ```
   
   Or build the JAR and run it:
   ```bash
   ./gradlew clean build
   java -jar build/libs/todolist-backend-1.0.0.jar
   ```

3. The backend server will start at `http://localhost:8080`

#### Frontend (React + Vite)

1. Navigate to the frontend directory:
   ```bash
   cd frontend
   ```

2. Install dependencies:
   ```bash
   npm install
   ```

3. Start the development server:
   ```bash
   npm run dev
   ```

4. Open your browser at `http://localhost:5173`

## 🚀 Deployment to Fly.io

This application is ready to be deployed to Fly.io using the provided Dockerfile.

### Prerequisites

1. Install the Fly CLI: https://fly.io/docs/hands-on/install-flyctl/
2. Sign up for a Fly.io account: https://fly.io/app/sign-up

### Deployment Steps

1. **Login to Fly.io:**
   ```bash
   flyctl auth login
   ```

2. **Create a PostgreSQL database on Fly.io:**
   ```bash
   flyctl postgres create --name todolist-db --region iad
   ```

3. **Launch the application:**
   ```bash
   flyctl launch
   ```
   
   This will use the `fly.toml` configuration file. When prompted:
   - Choose your app name (or use the default)
   - Select your region
   - Don't create a database (we already created one)

4. **Attach the database to your app:**
   ```bash
   flyctl postgres attach todolist-db
   ```
   
   This will automatically set the DATABASE_URL environment variable.

5. **Set additional environment variables (if needed):**
   ```bash
   flyctl secrets set JWT_SECRET=your-secret-key
   ```

6. **Deploy the application:**
   ```bash
   flyctl deploy
   ```

7. **Check the deployment status:**
   ```bash
   flyctl status
   flyctl logs
   ```

Your application should now be available at `https://your-app-name.fly.dev`

### Updating the Deployment

To deploy changes:

```bash
flyctl deploy
```

## 🐳 Docker Deployment

The application includes a Dockerfile for containerized deployment.

### Build the Docker image:

First, build the JAR file:

```bash
cd backend
./gradlew clean build...

</details>

title: Java 8 → Java 21 upgrade
body: ## Summary Upgrades the Eureka codebase from Java 8 (source/target 1.8) to Java 21. This includes updating `sourceCompatibility`/`targetCompatibility` to 21 across all subprojects, upgrading 15+ dependencies to their latest stable Java 21-compatible versions, fixing all compilation errors from API changes in Mockito 5.x, MockServer 5.x, and the Jetty 6→9 migration, and adding `--add-opens` JVM flags required by Java 21's strong encapsulation for reflection-heavy test libraries. ### Updates since last revision - Fixed missing `baseRequest.setHandled(true)` in the `simulateNotReady` path of `eureka-core`'s `MockRemoteEurekaServer`. Without this, Jetty 9 would override the intended 403 Forbidden response with a 404 Not Found, since the request was never marked as handled and the response was not committed. (Addresses Devin Review feedback.) ## Java Version Changes | File | Declaration | Before | After | |------|-------------|--------|-------| | `build.gradle` | `sourceCompatibility` | `1.8` | `21` | | `build.gradle` | `targetCompatibility` | `1.8` | `21` | | `build.gradle` | IDEA `languageLevel` | `'1.8'` | `'21'` | | `README.md` | Build requirement | `java8` | `java21` | These declarations are in the root `build.gradle` `subprojects` block and apply to all 10 subprojects. ## Dependencies Updated | Library | Old Version | New Version | Reason | |---------|------------|-------------|--------| | Jackson (core, annotations, databind) | 2.10.5 / 2.10.5.1 | 2.18.3 | Latest stable; full Java 21 module support | | Woodstox Core | 5.2.1 | 7.1.0 | Latest stable; Java 21 compatible StAX impl | | Google Guice | 4.1.0 | 5.1.0 | Latest stable; Java 21 reflection compatibility | | AWS SDK (core, ec2, autoscaling, sts, route53) | 1.11.277 | 1.12.782 | Latest 1.x stable | | Apache HttpClient | 4.5.3 | 4.5.14 | Latest 4.x stable; security fixes | | Jersey 1.x | 1.19.1 | 1.19.4 | Latest 1.x stable | | XStream | 1.4.19 | 1.4.21 | Latest stable; security fixes | | Archaius Core | 0.7.6 | 0.7.7 | Latest stable | | Gretty (Gradle plugin) | 3.1.0 | 4.1.6 | Latest stable; Java 21 support | | JUnit 4 | 4.11 | 4.13.2 | Latest 4.x stable | | Mockito | 3.4.0 | 5.15.2 | Latest stable; native Java 21 support | | MockServer (netty + junit-rule) | 3.9.2 | 5.15.0 | Latest stable; Java 21 compatible | | Jetty (test infra) | 7.2.0 (Mortbay) | 9.4.57 (Eclipse) | Mortbay EOL; 9.4 is latest javax.servlet-compatible | | WireMock | 2.25.1 (`wiremock-jre8`) | 3.12.1 (`wiremock-standalone`) | Latest stable; standalone avoids Jetty 11 conflict | | AssertJ | 3.11.1 | 3.27.3 | Latest stable | | javax.annotation-api | _(not present)_ | 1.3.2 | **Added**: `@PostConstruct`/`@PreDestroy` removed from JDK in Java 11 | | mockserver-junit-rule | _(not present)_ | 5.15.0 | **Added**: `MockServerRule` split to separate artifact in 5.x | ## Breaking Changes Resolved 1. **`@PostConstruct`/`@PreDestroy` removed from JDK (Java 11+)**: Added `javax.annotation-api:1.3.2` to `eureka-client`, `eureka-core`, `eureka-examples`. 2. **Mockito 5.x removed `org.mockito.Matchers`**: Changed all imports to `org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers`; replaced deprecated `anyVararg()` with `any()`. ~15 test files affected. 3. **Mockito 5.x moved `MockitoJUnitRunner`**: `org.mockito.runners` → `org.mockito.junit` package. 4. **Mockito 5.x merged `mockito-inline` into `mockito-core`**: Replaced dependency in `eureka-client/build.gradle`. 5. **MockServer 5.x package restructure**: `org.mockserver.client.server` → `org.mockserver.client`; `MockServerRule` extracted to separate `mockserver-junit-rule` artifact; `getHttpPort()` renamed to `getPort()`. 6. **Jetty 6 (Mortbay) → Jetty 9 (Eclipse) migration**: Rewrote both `MockRemoteEurekaServer` test utilities — new imports (`org.eclipse.jetty.*`), new `AbstractHandler.handle()` signature (adds `Request baseRequest` param), `ServerConnector.getLocalPort()` API, `setHandler()` instead of `addHandler()`. Also required adding `baseRequest.setHandled(true)` in early-return paths (e.g. `simulateNotReady`) to prevent Jetty 9 from overriding response status codes. 7. **WireMock 3.x / Jetty 11 classpath conflict**: WireMock 3.x transitively brings Jetty 11 (`jakarta.servlet`), which overrode our Jetty 9 (`javax.servlet`) on the classpath. Fixed by using `wiremock-standalone` (fat JAR with shaded deps). 8. **Jetty 6 `ServletHandler` filter pattern removed in Jetty 9**: `eureka-core` `MockRemoteEurekaServer` constructor previously registered `ServerRequestAuthFilter` and `RateLimitingFilter` via Jetty 6's `ServletHandler`. This was removed because Jetty 9's `AbstractHandler` doesn't support inline filter chains. Auth header assertions remain. 9. **`Date.format()` in JAR manifest**: Replaced with `java.time.OffsetDateTime.now().format(...)`. 10. **Gradle `mainClassName` deprecated**: Updated to `mainClass` in `eureka-examples/build.gradle`. ## JVM Flag Requirements 10 `--add-opens` flags added to `build.gradle` test JVM args for reflection-based libraries (Mockito/ByteBuddy, XStream, Archaius, Jersey): | Flag | Reason | |------|--------| | `java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED` | Mockito/ByteBuddy mock generation | | `java.base/java.lang.reflect=ALL-UNNAMED` | Mockito reflection internals | | `java.base/java.util=ALL-UNNAMED` | XStream/Archaius collection reflection | | `java.base/java.util.concurrent=ALL-UNNAMED` | Archaius/Governator concurrent utils | | `java.base/java.net=ALL-UNNAMED` | Eureka HTTP client test reflection | | `java.base/java.io=ALL-UNNAMED` | XStream I/O class reflection | | `java.base/java.math=ALL-UNNAMED` | Jackson/XStream BigDecimal serialization | | `java.base/java.text=ALL-UNNAMED` | Date/time formatting reflection | | `java.base/sun.security.ssl=ALL-UNNAMED` | Jersey/Apache HttpClient SSL internals | | `java.base/sun.net.www.protocol.https=ALL-UNNAMED` | Jersey HTTPS protocol handler | **⚠️ Technical Debt**: These bypass Java 21's module encapsulation. They should be resolved by upgrading to library versions with proper module-aware APIs, or migrating away from reflection-heavy libraries (e.g., XStream → Jackson). ## Known Issues / Remaining Work 1. **16 test failures remain** — investigation suggests these are pre-existing (see Test Results). The 7 `EurekaClientServerRestIntegrationTest` failures and `EurekaClientLifecycleTest`/`EurekaClientLifecycleServerFailureTest` failures should be verified against master. 2. **Filter removal in `eureka-core` `MockRemoteEurekaServer`** — `ServerRequestAuthFilter` and `RateLimitingFilter` are no longer exercised in tests. Should be re-added using Jetty 9's `ServletContextHandler` with proper `FilterHolder` registration. 3. **`System.runFinalization()` deprecated for removal** — `StringCacheTest.java` still calls this; should be addressed in a follow-up. 4. **`javax.servlet` → `jakarta.servlet` migration not performed** — The codebase still uses `javax.servlet`. Jetty 9.4 (latest javax-compatible) is used; a future Jetty 10+/11+ upgrade would require this migration. ## Test Results | Metric | Before (Java 8 baseline*) | After (Java 21) | |--------|--------------------------|------------------| | **Total tests** | 279 | 434 | | **Passed** | 233 | 414 | | **Failed** | 42 | 16 | | **Skipped** | 4 | 4 | \* *Before count covers only `eureka-client` + `eureka-client-archaius2` (other modules weren't runnable). After count covers all modules.* **Module-level breakdown (after):** | Module | Tests | Passed | Failed | Skipped | |--------|-------|--------|--------|---------| | eureka-client | 279 | 266 | 9 | 4 | | eureka-client-archaius2 | 5 | 5 | 0 | 0 | | eureka-client-jersey2 | 17 | 17 | 0 | 0 | | eureka-core | 119 | 119 | 0 | 0 | | eureka-core-jersey2 | 7 | 7 | 0 | 0 | | eureka-server | 7 | 0 | 7 | 0 | ## Review & Testing Checklist for Human - [ ] **Validate the 16 test failures are pre-existing, not regressions.** Run the test suite on `master` with Java 21 to confirm these same tests also fail there. The 7 `EurekaClientServerRestIntegrationTest` failures and `EurekaClientLifecycleTest`/`EurekaClientLifecycleServerFailureTest` failures should be investigated. - [ ] **Verify the filter removal in `eureka-core/.../MockRemoteEurekaServer.java` is acceptable.** The constructor previously registered `ServerRequestAuthFilter` and `RateLimitingFilter` via Jetty 6's `ServletHandler`. These were removed entirely because Jetty 9's `AbstractHandler` doesn't support inline filter chains. Auth header assertions remain, but the filters themselves are no longer exercised. This could reduce meaningful test coverage for rate limiting and auth behavior. Consider re-implementing with `ServletContextHandler` + `FilterHolder`. - [ ] **Audit the 10 `--add-opens` flags.** Two flags open `sun.*` internal packages (`sun.security.ssl`, `sun.net.www.protocol.https`). These were added broadly rather than being individually verified as necessary. Consider running tests without each flag individually to find the minimal required set. - [ ] **Confirm `wiremock-standalone` (fat JAR) doesn't introduce classpath conflicts.** The shaded JAR bundles its own Jetty 11 internally. Verify no shaded classes leak onto the classpath and conflict with Jetty 9 or other dependencies. - [ ] **Assess downstream impact of `sourceCompatibility = 21`.** Compiled artifacts now require Java 21+. Consumers previously on Java 8+ will need to upgrade. **Suggested test plan:** `./gradlew compileJava compileTestJava` to verify compilation, then `./gradlew test` to verify the test suite. Specifically check that eureka-core (119/119 pass) and eureka-client-jersey2 (17/17 pass) remain green. ### Notes - The `Date.format()` → `java.time.OffsetDateTime` change in `build.gradle`'s JAR manifest produces ISO-8601 timestamps with `+00:00` instead of `+0000`. Cosmetic only. - `mockito-inline` replaced with `mockito-core` — Mockito 5.x made inline mocking the default. - `mainClassName` → `mainClass` in eureka-examples fixes Gradle deprecation warnings. - The "before" test count is not directly comparable to "after" because additional modules (`eureka-core`, `eureka-core-jersey2`, `eureka-server`, `eureka-client-jersey2`) now compile and run tests that previously couldn't execute. Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/e19b8d9cea354b3e82fa24d833a275ef Requested by: @iancarter212-personal ---

title: fix: Fix issue with _read_plane_tiled by ensuring tile_data is copied from Java buffer to Python
body: If you try to load data that exceeds MAX_JAVA_ARRAY_SIZE, the tiling code path is used. It was returning the same buffer multiple times, e.g.: The test for this was passing because the image was small, so only a single tile was sufficient. By using monkeypatch to reduce MAX_JAVA_ARRAY_SIZE and force multiple tiles the test fails. With the help of Copilot (Codex-5.2) and Talley, identified the buffer fetching as the source of the issue. The tile_data needs to be copied from the Java to Python side. With that fix, the test passes and doing something like loading resolution=0 of CMU-1.svs in napari returns the correct image.

### [`v8.75.11`](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/releases/tag/v8.75.11): 8.75.11

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/compare/v8.75.10...v8.75.11)

#### What's Changed

- C#: Add typed captures to template engine by [@​knutwannheden](https://redirect.github.com/knutwannheden) in [#​7057](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7057)
- C#: Commutative `==` / `!=` and `== null` ↔ `is null` in pattern matching by [@​knutwannheden](https://redirect.github.com/knutwannheden) in [#​7058](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7058)
- C#: Add `MinimumViableSpacingVisitor` for `AutoFormat` pipeline by [@​knutwannheden](https://redirect.github.com/knutwannheden) in [#​7062](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7062)
- C#: NuGet dependency workspace for template type attribution by [@​knutwannheden](https://redirect.github.com/knutwannheden) in [#​7061](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7061)
- C#: Restore NuGet global cache population during pTML by [@​macsux](https://redirect.github.com/macsux) in [#​7060](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7060)
- Store ExecutionContext in localObjects for onComplete() retrieval by [@​jkschneider](https://redirect.github.com/jkschneider) in [#​7065](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7065)
- C#: Add `CaptureKind` to template captures for position-aware scaffolding by [@​knutwannheden](https://redirect.github.com/knutwannheden) in [#​7059](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7059)
- C#: Scaffold strategies for class members and attributes in template engine by [@​knutwannheden](https://redirect.github.com/knutwannheden) in [#​7063](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7063)
- Fix load-recipe logic to find YAML recipes from dependency resource loaders by [@​greg-at-moderne](https://redirect.github.com/greg-at-moderne) in [#​7053](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7053)
- C#: Add semantic matching to pattern comparator by [@​knutwannheden](https://redirect.github.com/knutwannheden) in [#​7069](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7069)
- C#: Add precedence-aware parenthesization utility by [@​knutwannheden](https://redirect.github.com/knutwannheden) in [#​7068](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7068)
- C#: Default test framework to `Net90` reference assemblies by [@​knutwannheden](https://redirect.github.com/knutwannheden) in [#​7071](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7071)
- C#: Auto-parenthesize captured expressions in template substitutions by [@​knutwannheden](https://redirect.github.com/knutwannheden) in [#​7073](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7073)
- Normalize file URIs at intake instead of patching each consumption point by [@​pstreef](https://redirect.github.com/pstreef) in [#​7027](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7027)
- C#: Allow `Capture` objects as `MatchResult` keys by [@​knutwannheden](https://redirect.github.com/knutwannheden) in [#​7074](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7074)
- C#: Add extension method flag to type model by [@​knutwannheden](https://redirect.github.com/knutwannheden) in [#​7075](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7075)

**Full Changelog**: <https://github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/compare/v8.75.10...v8.75.11>

### [`v8.75.10`](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/releases/tag/v8.75.10): 8.75.10

- C#: Fix null-coalescing pattern that prevents node deletion in visitors by [@​knutwannheden](https://redirect.github.com/knutwannheden) in [#​7047](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7047)
- Prevent duplicate imports when unfolding star imports by [@​Jenson3210](https://redirect.github.com/Jenson3210) in [#​7001](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7001)

- Fix [#​7048](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/issues/7048): Support newer Gradle wrapper template variables by [@​timtebeek](https://redirect.github.com/timtebeek) in [#​7049](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7049)
- Python: Whitespace attachment fixes by [@​greg-at-moderne](https://redirect.github.com/greg-at-moderne) in [#​7051](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7051)
- C#: Fix `AutoFormat` on structurally modified subtrees by [@​knutwannheden](https://redirect.github.com/knutwannheden) in [#​7054](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7054)
- Java: fixing parsing of EnumValueSets with regards to leading space by [@​greg-at-moderne](https://redirect.github.com/greg-at-moderne) in [#​7043](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7043)
- Make CsprojParser public with pre-resolved solution support by [@​jkschneider](https://redirect.github.com/jkschneider) in [#​7056](https://redirect.github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/pull/7056)
