title: chore(deps): bump openrewrite.version from 8.78.3 to 8.78.4
body:
Bumps `openrewrite.version` from 8.78.3 to 8.78.4. Updates `org.openrewrite:rewrite-java` from 8.78.3 to 8.78.4
Release notes

Sourced from org.openrewrite:rewrite-java's releases.

8.78.4

What's Changed

  • Fix Javadoc @​see field reference resolution by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#7257
  • Go: load recipe modules as plugins for real execution by @​jkschneider in openrewrite/rewrite#7263
  • Go: automatically visit tree nodes in RPC queues with no onChange callback by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7264
  • Go: StatementExpression registered as a type by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7265
  • Go: Fix parsing of comments in empty argument list by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7267
  • Exclude the version txt file from the build cache by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7266
  • Go: Fix type cast in RPC, literals are not always primitive in Go by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7268
  • Go: Run go native tests as part of the build by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7270
  • Go: fix more cases of comments parsing in empty enumerations by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7269
  • Declare Gradle inputs to tasks for rewrite-csharp and rewrite-python by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7272
  • Go: Fix RPC serialization for varargs by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7271
  • Go: Fix parsing of a trailing comma in argument list by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7273

Full Changelog: https://github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/compare/v8.78.3...v8.78.4

Commits
  • d934b7b Fix parsing of a trailing comma in argument list (#7273)
  • f6682ed Scala parser: map This, InterpolatedString, ForEachLoop, and TypeAscription t...
  • d51d23f Go: Fix RPC serialization for varargs (#7271)
  • fbaf874 Declare Gradle inputs to tasks (#7272)
  • ab2af5d Fix more cases of comments in empty enumerations (#7269)
  • 341d1aa Run go native tests too (#7270)
  • 2a229dd Fix type cast, literals are not always primitive in Go (#7268)
  • 7265ee0 Exclude the version txt file from the build cache (#7266)
  • 2edcb6f Fix parsing of comments in empty arg list (#7267)
  • 942105a StatementExpression registered (#7265)
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

title: chore(deps): bump openrewrite.version from 8.78.6 to 8.79.1
body:
Bumps `openrewrite.version` from 8.78.6 to 8.79.1. Updates `org.openrewrite:rewrite-java` from 8.78.6 to 8.79.1
Release notes

Sourced from org.openrewrite:rewrite-java's releases.

v8.79.1

What's Changed

  • C#: Improve csproj manipulation and add reattestation by @​macsux in openrewrite/rewrite#7316
  • XmlParser: case-insensitive accept for nuget.config by @​steve-aom-elliott in openrewrite/rewrite#7317
  • C#: Remove REWRITE_SOURCE_PATH env var from RPC launcher by @​macsux in openrewrite/rewrite#7322
  • Treat all packages listed in requirements.txt as direct dependencies by @​Jenson3210 in openrewrite/rewrite#7320
  • Parse all requirements files in requirements-only Python projects by @​Jenson3210 in openrewrite/rewrite#7323

Full Changelog: https://github.com/openrewrite/rewrite/compare/v8.79.0...v8.79.1

8.79.0

What's Changed

  • Go: Fix how generics (parametrized types) are parsed by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7294
  • Fix DTD ENTITY parsing when quoted values contain angle brackets by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#7280
  • Scala: ExpressionStatements by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7296
  • C#: Fix RPC serialization of XmlDoc comments by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7297
  • Fix NPE in UpgradeDependencyVersion ext property upgrade by @​Jenson3210 in openrewrite/rewrite#7299
  • Scala: fix parsing of pattern matching with case conditions by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7298
  • Go: fix parsing of multi-parameter generics by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7300
  • Add UsePropertyAssignmentSyntax recipe for Gradle (#5828) by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#7286
  • Scala: parsing to support multiline case blocks with no {} braces by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7301
  • Support META-INF/services files in ChangePackage and ChangeType by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#7282
  • Preserve whitespace in DTD internal subset markup declarations by @​Jenson3210 in openrewrite/rewrite#7302
  • Fix Groovy as-style type casts losing space around as keyword by @​timtebeek in openrewrite/rewrite#7303
  • Python: fix for not crashing with empty files by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7304
  • Scala: fix whitespace parsing of type casts by @​greg-at-moderne in openrewrite/rewrite#7306

title: Updating Unet.py with the new UnetSocket API
body:
# Modernising unetpy

Unetpy implementation had diverged from the original design over the years. This PR rewrites unetpy to make it use more modern python features and tools. It reorganises the unetpy implementation to be similar to the unetjs implementation, but still tries to keep things idiomatic to python.

## Key Improvements

* **Updated UnetSocket support**
  Support for the updated Unet Socket API introduced in UnetStack v6. Initially added in https://github.com/org-arl/unetsockets/pull/8

* **Modern packaging**
  Migrated to `pyproject.toml` for project metadata and build configuration.

* **Testing against a live container**
  Added tests for socket features against a Unet simulator

* **Better developer ergonomics**

  * Docstrings and type hints across most APIs
  * Context manager support: `with UnetSocket('localhost', 1100) as socket:`
  * Cleaner logging (no spurious messages, lowered default levels)

* **Documentation**
  Automated API docs generation based on docstrings published to readme files.

title: Restructure repo: move Python to python/, add Java 21 port in java/
body:
Splits the single-file Python tool into a proper `python/` subdirectory and adds a parallel Java 21 implementation in its own `java/` directory, each with full build tooling and cross-platform scripts.

## Python (`python/`)
- Moved `xmlcompare.py`, `tests/`, `samples/`, `requirements.txt` from repo root into `python/`
- Added `build.sh/.bat/.ps1` — creates venv, installs deps, runs pytest
- Added `run.sh/.bat/.ps1` — activates venv and delegates to `xmlcompare.py`

## Java (`java/`)
- Full port of all comparison logic in `com.xmlcompare` package: `CompareOptions`, `Difference`, `XmlCompare`, `Main`
- DOM-based XML parsing (`javax.xml`), picocli for CLI, Jackson for JSON/YAML config
- Identical feature set: numeric tolerance, namespace stripping, ordered/unordered children, skip-keys/pattern, XPath filter, text/JSON/HTML output, `--config` file, exit codes 0/1/2
- Dual build: Gradle (`build.gradle` + wrapper) and Maven (`pom.xml` with shade plugin for fat JAR)
- Added `build.sh/.bat/.ps1` — runs both Gradle and Maven builds
- Added `run.sh/.bat/.ps1` — invokes the fat JAR via `java -jar`
- 55 JUnit 5 tests covering all major code paths

## Docs & housekeeping
- Root `README.md` rewritten to describe the two-language layout with quick-start commands for each
- `.gitignore` updated: Python `.venv/`, Java `build/`/`target/` per-project rather than repo-global `build/`

```bash
# Python
cd python && ./build.sh
./run.sh --files samples/orders_expected.xml samples/orders_actual_diff.xml --output-format json

# Java
cd java && ./build.sh
./run.sh --files samples/orders_expected.xml samples/orders_actual_diff.xml --output-format json
```

title: Port PCTSEA Java tool to Python CLI application with full tests
body:
This pull request introduces the initial implementation of the PCTSEA (Proteomics Cell Type Single-cell Enrichment Analysis) Python package. It adds the core CLI, configuration, cell type ontology logic, and project setup necessary for running the analysis pipeline. The most important changes are grouped below.

**Project setup and configuration:**

* Added `pyproject.toml` with project metadata, dependencies, build configuration, and development tools, ensuring the package is ready for installation and development. (`pctsea-python/pyproject.toml`)
* Added `.gitignore` and `.python-version` files to manage Python environment and versioning. (`pctsea-python/.gitignore`, `.python-version`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-207e4ab656403ee1ebd790cd2dd77356efba7b6ded15b49bd92fc5c82f94ada2R1-R8) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-02b16bdc82ea3d804c824a6ff312f09397e04f687fdc2b1167df94d7a601763eR1)

**Core package and CLI:**

* Implemented the main CLI entry point in `cli.py`, enabling users to run the PCTSEA pipeline with various options for scoring, thresholds, database connection, and output. (`pctsea-python/src/pctsea/cli.py`)
* Added package initialization and versioning in `__init__.py`. (`pctsea-python/src/pctsea/__init__.py`)

**Cell type ontology and configuration:**

* Implemented hierarchical cell type mapping, typo correction, and normalization logic in `cell_types.py`, supporting robust cell type resolution and classification. (`pctsea-python/src/pctsea/cell_types.py`)
* Defined enums and constants for scoring methods, cell type hierarchy, and input data types in `config.py`, centralizing configuration for the analysis pipeline. (`pctsea-python/src/pctsea/config.py`)

title: Fix loadflow provider parameters
body:
**Please check if the PR fulfills these requirements**
<!-- please use `'[x]'` to check the checkboxes, or submit the PR and then click the checkboxes -->
- [x] The commit message follows our guidelines
- [x] Tests for the changes have been added (for bug fixes / features)

- [ ] A PR or issue has been opened in all impacted repositories (if any)


**Does this PR already have an issue describing the problem?**
<!-- If so, link to this issue using `'Fixes #XXX'` and skip the rest -->
Fixes #1153, #1144


**What is the new behavior (if this is a feature change)?**
When creating loadflow Parameters, we now always pass from java to python the provider parameters associated with the default provider chosen in pypowsybl configuration (which is by default OpenLoadFlow, and can be set with set_default_provider method)

This way :
- a json parameters file created with OLF specific parameters will be correctly read (if the default provider is still OLF)
-  when default parameters are defined in java using the LoadFlowDefaultParametersLoader mecanism, users will see that the values corresponding to their default provider are set. 

**Does this PR introduce a breaking change or deprecate an API?**
- [ ] Yes
- [x] No

Adds an initial Java file to the otherwise empty repository.

### Changes
- **`HelloWorld.java`** — minimal `main` entry point that prints `"Hello, World!"`
- **`.gitignore`** — excludes compiled `*.class` artifacts from version control


title: docs: Reorganize documentation structure
body:
## Summary

Reorganizes documentation structure to improve discoverability and separate development concepts from operational concerns.

## Changes

### New Operations Guide
- Created `operations-guide-index.md` as landing page
- Moved configuration, authentication, security, LLM/embedding providers, vector store backends from Developer Guide

### SDK Documentation
- **Python SDK**: Complete rewrite with accurate API examples, `MemoryClientConfig` usage, tool integration, filter reference
- **TypeScript SDK**: New documentation (358 lines)
- **Java SDK**: New documentation (321 lines)
- All SDKs now under API Reference → Client SDKs

### Cleanup
- Consolidated AWS Bedrock content into `llm-providers.md`, deleted duplicate `aws-bedrock.md`
- Fixed broken anchor link in `langchain-integration.md`
- Updated `mkdocs.yml` navigation

### Navigation Structure
```
Developer Guide: Memory concepts, integration patterns, LangChain
Operations Guide: Configuration, Auth, Security, Providers
API Reference: REST, MCP, CLI, Client SDKs (Python, TypeScript, Java)
```

## Testing
- `mkdocs build --strict` passes
- All grammarian style checks pass (Excellent ratings)

## Notes
- `python-sdk-index.md` is now orphaned (not in nav) - can be deleted in follow-up if desired

title: Add lsp-java support for Java mode
Switch from pyenv to deadsnakes PPA for Python management
body:
Resolves #326

## Summary
Replace pyenv-based Python installation with system-level packages from deadsnakes PPA, using update-alternatives for version switching.

## Changes

### packer/base/install/install_python.sh
- Complete rewrite to use deadsnakes PPA instead of pyenv
- Installs Python 2.7 and 3.10 via apt packages
- Configures update-alternatives with proper priorities (2.7=1, 3.10=2)
- Includes Python 2.7 pip support for Cassandra 3.x compatibility
- Removes .bash_profile modifications (no longer needed)

### packer/cassandra/bin/use-cassandra  
- Replace pyenv global command with update-alternatives --set
- Adds validation to ensure requested version is installed
- Extracts major.minor version from full version string
- Consistent with existing Java version management pattern

### packer/base/base.pkr.hcl
- Update comment to reflect new approach

## Benefits
- Faster installation (no compilation required)
- System-integrated packages with automatic security updates
- Consistent with existing Java version management using update-alternatives
- Reduced dependencies (no build tools needed just for Python)
- Better caching through apt package mirrors

## Testing
- All packer Docker tests pass (testPackerBase, testPackerCassandra)
- Python 2.7 and 3.10 installation verified
- Version switching logic validated
- iostat-tool installation working correctly

## Notes
- This changes the Python installation method in AMIs
- Existing pyenv-based AMIs will not be compatible
- New AMIs will use deadsnakes PPA packages
- Python 2.7 pip included for Cassandra 3.x compatibility

title: feat: port Strops class from Java to Python
body:
## Summary
Port the Strops string operations class from Java to Python, implementing instance-based methods for string reversal and palindrome detection with full type hints and comprehensive documentation.

## Changes
- Create `Strops` class with instance methods `reverse()` and `is_palindrome()`
- Implement efficient string operations using Pythonic slicing (O(n) time complexity)
- Add type hints for all method signatures
- Include TypeError handling for None inputs
- Handle edge cases: empty strings reverse to empty and are palindromes
- Add Google-style docstrings with complexity analysis and usage examples

## Testing
- [ ] Verify `reverse()` works with normal, empty, and special character strings
- [ ] Verify `is_palindrome()` correctly identifies palindromes and non-palindromes
- [ ] Verify TypeError is raised when None is passed to either method

---
**Related:** Part of strings module Java-to-Python translation initiative

title: docs(agent-auth): add Node.js SDK tabs and fix SDK method references
body:
## Summary

- Added Node.js SDK tabs to all Agent Auth pages (`quickstart.mdx`, `connected-accounts.mdx`, `tools/authorize.mdx`, `tools/execute.mdx`) using real methods from the `agent-auth-apis` branch (`@scalekit-sdk/node@2.2.0-beta.1`)
- Replaced fictional `agentConnect.accounts.*` methods in `connected-accounts.mdx` with real SDK calls for both Python and Node.js
- Restored `agent-auth/connected-accounts` in the sidebar (was previously commented out)
- Fixed Python `execute_tool` parameter: `tool=` → `tool_name=`
- Fixed status monitoring sections to use read-only lookups (`get_connected_account` / `getConnectedAccountByIdentifier`) instead of mutating `get_or_create` calls
- Added beta version `<Aside>` notes on pages using Node.js Agent Auth methods
- Fixed `tools/authorize.mdx` Python variable: `connect.` → `actions.`
- Added missing `python-dotenv` and `requests` to pip install in quickstart; added `import requests` to the init block
- Removed unused `Card`/`CardGrid` imports from component import lines

## Why

The Agent Auth docs were using a mix of fictional SDK methods and outdated Node.js exclusions. The Node.js SDK methods (`connectedAccounts.*`, `tools.*`) are now available in the `agent-auth-apis` beta branch. This PR aligns all documentation with the actual SDK source for both Python (`feature/api-tokens`) and Node.js (`agent-auth-apis`).

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm --filter scalekit-docs build` passes
- [x] Pre-push hook build passes
- [ ] Verify all Agent Auth pages render with Python + Node.js tabs
- [ ] Verify `agent-auth/connected-accounts` is visible in the sidebar
- [ ] Smoke-test Python snippets against `scalekit-sdk-python` `feature/api-tokens` branch
- [ ] Smoke-test Node.js snippets against `scalekit-sdk-node` `agent-auth-apis` branch

Blitzy: Node.js-to-Flask Rewrite — Blocked: Empty Repository (No Source Code)
body:
## Summary

This branch was created in response to a request to rewrite a Node.js server application into Python 3 using Flask. After comprehensive investigation and validation, the agents confirmed that **the repository contains no application source code** — only a single placeholder `README.md` file (content: `# 24Feb_1`). There is no Node.js server code, no `package.json`, no route definitions, no middleware, no database models, and no configuration files of any kind.

The accompanying Technical Specification describes a Java 21-based Sonatype Nexus Repository system, which is unrelated to the user's Node.js-to-Flask migration request.

## Key Findings
- **Repository state:** Empty — 1 file (`README.md`, 9 bytes), 1 commit (`49fb77f Initial commit`)
- **All branches inspected:** `main`, `Newdad`, `Thisnew`, `gsdjgsdjfds`, `blitzy-33a3b9ad` — all contain only `README.md`
- **Agent work:** Investigation, validation, and root cause analysis completed; zero implementation possible
- **Files changed:** 0 files created, modified, or deleted
- **Blocking dependency:** The Node.js server source code must be committed to the repository before any rewrite work can proceed

## Required User Action
The user must provide the Node.js server source code (via commit, attachment, or external reference) to unblock this project.

title: Rewrite lnky URL shortener from Go to Python/FastAPI
body:
Rewrites the URL shortener service in Python with FastAPI while maintaining identical behavior. Designed for AWS AppRunner deployment with local development support via DynamoDB Local.

## Core Application (`main.py`)
- FastAPI app with async handlers for `/api/shorten`, `/{short_id}` redirect, `/api/stats/{short_id}`
- Background asyncio worker for non-blocking analytics storage
- DynamoDB integration with custom endpoint support for local development
- Proxy-aware URL generation via `X-Forwarded-Host`/`X-Forwarded-Proto` headers

## Environment Variables
- Existing: `AWS_REGION`, `AWS_ENDPOINT`, `URLS_TABLE`, `ANALYTICS_TABLE`, `PORT`
- New feature flags: `ENABLE_SHORTEN`, `ENABLE_REDIRECT`, `ENABLE_STATS` (default: true)

## Docker
- Simplified `Dockerfile` using `python:3.12-slim`
- Refactored `docker-compose.yml` with dedicated DynamoDB table setup container

## Tests (`test_main.py`)
- Integration tests against DynamoDB Local
- Covers URL shortening, redirects, analytics, conflict detection, validation
- Performance and concurrency tests (marked `slow`)

```bash
# Local development
docker run -p 8000:8000 amazon/dynamodb-local
AWS_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:8000 python -m uvicorn main:app

# Run tests
docker-compose up -d
pytest -v test_main.py
```

title: NO-SNOW: Improve ConfigManager implementation
body:
## Summary

- Add `bool_value` support to `ConfigSetting` protobuf message
- Add `ConfigGetPaths` RPC so Python can resolve config file locations from sf_core
- Pass explicit file paths (`config_file`, `connections_file`) in `ConfigLoadAllSectionsRequest` so Python controls which files are read
- Rewrite Python `ConfigManager` to delegate **all** file I/O, TOML parsing, and permission checks to sf_core (removes `tomlkit`/`platformdirs` dependency from Python side)
- Add error translation from Rust core `DriverException` to Python `ConfigManagerError`/`ConfigSourceError`
- Fix `MissingConfigOptionError` hierarchy (now extends `ConfigSourceError`)
- Add `constants.py` re-exporting `CONFIG_FILE` and `CONNECTIONS_FILE`
- Handle root-level TOML keys in `config_manager.rs`
- Fix `toml_loader` to handle `try_exists()` errors gracefully
- Add `test_config_manager_old_tests.py` for backward compatibility coverage

Split from turbaszek-backcompat-3 — ConfigManager-only changes.

## Test plan
- [ ] Unit tests pass: `python/tests/unit/test_config_manager.py`
- [ ] Integration tests pass: `python/tests/integ/config/`
- [ ] Rust config_manager tests pass
- [ ] Verify config file path resolution works across platforms

## 🌿 feat: Migrate Bloom API from Java/Spring Boot to Python/FastAPI

### What
Complete backend migration from Java 17 + Spring Boot 3.4.4 to Python + FastAPI.
All 14 API endpoints preserved with identical request/response contracts.

### Why
- Align with VibeSpace LLC technology standards (Python-first for consumer products)
- Faster iteration speed for Sprint 2 feature development
- Native async support for future AI integration features
- Reuse VibeConnect infrastructure patterns

### Changes
- **Removed**: Java/Spring Boot backend (pom.xml, all .java files, Maven wrapper)
- **Added**: FastAPI application with SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic migrations
- **Preserved**: All API endpoints, response shapes, auth flow, database schema
- **Fixed**: Profile update bug (creating profile without user relationship)

title: refactor(hitl): simplify HITL approval flow with single-source-of-truth data model
body:
## Summary
Eliminate HITL approval data duplication by making `messages[].tool_calls` the single source of truth. Replace fuzzy interrupt matching with direct `tool_call_id` injection via LangChain's `InjectedToolCallId`, and compute `pending_approvals` server-side from stored messages rather than merging client-reported state.

## Context
The HITL approval flow had accumulated redundant data paths: flat `tool_calls` lists duplicated data already present in messages, `ApprovalLifecycleState` tracked state the server could derive, and the Python agent-runner used a 4-tier fuzzy matching chain to correlate interrupts with tool calls. This made the system fragile and hard to reason about.

## Changes
**Proto data model (T02)**
- Delete `ApprovalLifecycleState` enum and fields 9–12 from `PendingApproval`
- Remove flat `tool_calls` from `AgentExecutionStatus` and `SubAgentExecution`
- Remove `pending_approvals` from `SubAgentExecution`
- Add `args_preview` to `ToolCall` and `WorkflowPendingApproval` wrapper type

**Python agent-runner (T01, T03)**
- Inject `tool_call_id` via `InjectedToolCallId` on all 6 tool wrappers
- Replace flat `tool_calls` list with `_tool_call_index` holding direct protobuf references
- Delete `ApprovalStateManager`, `InterruptCapture`, and `CheckpointFallback` (~2,100 lines removed)
- Simplify resume path from ~140 to ~40 lines

**Go server (T05)**
- New `ComputePendingApprovals` function (replaces merge-based approach)
- `SubmitApproval`: message-based tool call lookup with recompute after recording
- `UpdateStatus`: replace merge with compute

**React SDK (T06)**
- Remove `ApprovalLifecycleState` import, exponential-backoff polling, and timer-based staleness recovery
- Simplify dismissed state from `Map<string, number>` to `Set<string>`

**CLI (T05)**
- `collectToolCallsFromMessages` helper for all tool call access
- Adapt to `WorkflowPendingApproval` type

**Tests (T07)**
- Rewrite Go approval tests: 4 old → 9 new covering message-based lookup, recomputation, sub-agent isolation
- Delete `test_approval_resume.py`, rewrite `test_hitl_contracts.py` (10 tests) and `test_status_builder.py` (266 tests)

## Implementation notes
- Stubs regenerated for Go, Java, Python, TypeScript across `apis/stubs/`, `mcp-server/proto/`, and `sdk/go/proto/`
- Proto field numbers reserved to prevent wire-format conflicts

## Breaking changes
- `ApprovalLifecycleState` enum deleted — consumers must remove references
- Flat `tool_calls` and sub-agent `pending_approvals` removed from proto — clients must read from `messages[].tool_calls`
- `PendingApproval` fields 9–12 removed and reserved

## Test plan
- All Python tests pass: graphton 1,209, agent-runner 1,351
- Go `make check` clean
- React SDK tests updated and passing
- 276 total rewritten Python HITL tests, 9 new Go contract tests

## Risks
- Downstream consumers of the deleted proto fields will break until updated
- Feature branch; merged together with stigmer-cloud counterpart

## Checklist
- [x] Docs updated (changelogs, design decisions, project checkpoints)
- [x] Tests added/updated (comprehensive rewrite across Go, Python, React)
- [ ] Backward compatible (breaking proto changes — coordinated release required)

title: fix(comsol): add run_file so /run path works end-to-end
body:
## Summary

- `ComsolDriver.run_file()` was missing, so `sim.runner.execute_script(driver=...)` crashed with `AttributeError` for every COMSOL script. This regressed (latently) in the unified DriverProtocol refactor (f428ab6), which routed COMSOL through the one-shot `/run` path but never added the execution method.
- Add `run_file()` matching the PyFluent driver's pattern (shell out to `sys.executable`).
- Replace a placebo `TestRunFile` test with two real ones — the old test monkeypatched `execute_script` then called `execute_script` directly, so it never touched the driver.

## Root cause

`server.py /run` → `runner.execute_script(driver=driver)` → `driver.run_file(script)` (see `src/sim/runner.py:47`). Before this PR, `ComsolDriver` had no `run_file`, so every call raised `AttributeError: 'ComsolDriver' object has no attribute 'run_file'`.

## Changes

- `src/sim/drivers/comsol/driver.py` — add `run_file(self, script)` that delegates to `run_subprocess([sys.executable, str(script)], ...)`. Keeps the driver SDK-free at module load; the subprocess inherits whichever `mph`/JPype/Java is present in the env.
- `tests/test_comsol_driver.py` — rewrite `TestRunFile`:
  - `test_run_file_invokes_python_subprocess`: asserts the subprocess command starts with `[sys.executable, script]`.
  - `test_run_file_routes_through_execute_script`: asserts `runner.execute_script(driver=driver)` completes without `AttributeError` — the exact integration path that was broken.

## Test plan

- [x] Offline suite: 84 passed on macOS (was 83; +1 new real test).
- [x] Live on Windows (win1) with COMSOL 6.4.0.293 + `mph` 1.3.1 + JPype 1.7.0: `driver.run_file(script)` → `mph.start()` boots the Java client → `exit_code=0`, stdout `{"ok": true, "java_version": "6.4", "models": []}`.
- [ ] Reviewer: sanity-check the subprocess pattern matches PyFluent (`src/sim/drivers/fluent/driver.py:264`).

title: 65 refactor drivecoordinator and add drive to climb state
body:
This pull request introduces a new TypeScript-based auto-generation system for robot autonomous routines, including infrastructure for compiling, registering, and serializing auto actions, as well as updates to field and drive constants. The most important changes are grouped below:

**Auto Generator System Implementation**

* Added a new `auto_generator` directory with TypeScript configuration (`tsconfig.json`), build scripts (`compile_autos.mjs`, `run_autos.mjs`), and supporting files for compiling and running auto routines. This includes a sample auto routine (`newTestAuto.ts`) and utility modules (`AutoLib.ts`, `Shorthands.ts`). [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-839e9c143a1f4d1b54f0a2ec56d295585f773846c5c0078655c3b3d6fdd7b450R1-R28) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-92a33988d0a6d375e363efd86b5220afe13d3785fcb18b7a499eaa0d4026e4eaR1-R48) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-6f6e133a914fc43b08ce933a7298054234cdfea8730ba66ee686cbf58152acd7R1-R62) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-512c88f911be4172d2514bc12301e45d1f245df9a84f04c574ae12e3f432e737R1-R70) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-3f13d8d0193694f84e8389fa2d363c566e6e455cc3bf5aa105194612edd65bdcR1-R19) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-7b685dfd1624fff9943ceff5ac1bed50e2ea25e516fd2a3e5970cab7fda86a17R1-R17) [[7]](diffhunk://#diff-4bc385d22ab2b19553fff5781ecb60bfbed3913eebb52a1b55924a2eb822a910R1-R18)
* Introduced a comprehensive set of classes in `AutoAction.ts` to represent auto actions, targets, profiles, gains, and related types for serialization and execution.

**Auto Serialization and Output**

* The system now generates a single `Autos.json` file in `src/main/deploy` containing serialized auto routines, such as the "Random Auto" sequence for testing.

**Field and Drive Constants Updates**

* Updated `DriveConstants.json` files to include `defaultAutoPilotHeadingGains`, providing default PID gains for autopilot heading control in both competition and test drivebase configurations. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-80405417fa8b59ae33b8cec0b191cc11a5ea3cdfb06e034d88e18dc7a46bd9f0R61-R69) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-4a044d751d5be430bcc18af7cc1512a8d25b884b34148cf52dc3b9d365ce9e6fR61-R69)
* Added climb locations to `BlueFieldLocations.json` and `RedFieldLocations.json`, specifying translation and rotation for left and right climb positions. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-71f785d87add7ef36c59e71a58820e99325ef4109978f130746af77dc87e596dR11-R28) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-71f2e594c19f11fdb444b418961781e057eda337ac1479d6027a76eeda800f55R11-R28)

**Vision and Controller Configuration**

* Adjusted vision gain constants in `VisionConstants.json` for the test drivebase, increasing the standard deviation factors.
* Added a sample vision configuration file (`vision-sample.json`) and updated controller bindings in `controllers-xbox.json` to include a new test command. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-bcae95158a58c8c002d4eb88beed086ff16420c9a8bc2ca07229e838b3e1afe3R1-R52) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-bb107cf6a856a1a1e6c5c3478fa275d35c3d0f4855d993023ef869389b504199R64-R71)

**Miscellaneous**

* Added `.gitignore` entries for `node_modules` and `dist` to the `auto_generator` directory.

title: divin-fix: remediate CAST issues in WebgoatProperties.java (4 issues)
body: # divin-fix: remediate CAST issues in WebgoatProperties.java ## Summary Automated CAST Imaging Express remediation for `WebgoatProperties.java`. Two categories of changes: 1. **`e.printStackTrace()` → `System.err.println("Error: " + e.getMessage())`** — 1 location in `main()` catch block. Addresses CAST `printStackTrace` anti-pattern (stack trace leakage). 2. **`// CAST: Consider using a proper logging framework`** — 3 comment annotations above `System.out.println` calls (constructor, `main()` × 2). **No behavioral change** for these. ⚠️ The diff appears as a full-file rewrite (~123 lines removed, ~126 added) because the automated regex script rewrote the file. Only the 4 edits above are intentional. ## Review & Testing Checklist for Human - [ ] **Verify no accidental content/logic changes from the full-file rewrite**: GitHub's diff shows a complete rewrite. Run `diff -bw` between this branch and `WebGoat_5.2_Devin` to confirm only the 4 intended edits exist. In particular, verify `getIntProperty()`, `getBooleanProperty()`, `trimLesson()`, and the `FileInputStream` constructor logic are byte-identical to the base. - [ ] **Evaluate `getMessage()` returning null in `main()`**: The original `e.printStackTrace()` provided a full stack trace. The replacement `System.err.println("Error: " + e.getMessage())` loses all stack context, and `getMessage()` can return `null` for some `IOException` subtypes, yielding `"Error: null"`. Decide if this diagnostic regression is acceptable for the `main()` test harness. - [ ] **Compile the file** (`javac -Xlint`) — no compilation was performed before this commit. Confirm no encoding corruption was introduced by the regex rewrite. ### Notes - Applied via automated Python regex script — no manual review or compilation was performed before commit. - The 3 `System.out.println` calls were annotated with comments but **not replaced** — this PR makes zero behavioral change for those lines. - Pre-existing bug: `main()` can NPE on `properties.getProperty(...)` if the constructor throws — not introduced by this PR. - No logging framework exists in this codebase; `System.err.println` is the best available substitute without adding a dependency. Link to Devin run: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/d40f9f0f9c5e43ddb8a405cd743e5f0b Requested by: @theabhisheksinha ---

title: Extract LXST-kt as standalone library with full audio pipeline
body:
## Summary

- **Extracts the `:lxst` module into a standalone library** ([LXST-kt](https://github.com/torlando-tech/LXST-kt)) consumed via git submodule + Gradle composite build
- **Builds the complete Kotlin audio pipeline** (Phases 7–11): codecs, sources/sinks, mixer, network bridge, telephony state machine
- **Removes dead Python LXST dependencies** (numpy, pycodec2, chaquopy_audio_backend) — Python is only used for Reticulum/LXMF transport now
- **Makes LXST-kt transport-agnostic** — ready to swap Python Reticulum for a future Kotlin Reticulum with no refactoring

## What's included

### Kotlin Audio Pipeline (Phases 7–11)
- **Phase 7 — Codec Foundation**: Opus (9 profiles) and Codec2 (7 modes) with JNI, MIT-licensed wrappers
- **Phase 8 — Audio Sources & Sinks**: LineSource (mic), LineSink (speaker), float32/int16 conversion
- **Phase 9 — Mixer & Pipeline**: Multi-source mixer, ToneSource (382Hz dial tone), Pipeline orchestration
- **Phase 10 — Network Bridge**: PacketRouter (outbound/inbound packets + signalling), AudioPacketHandler interface
- **Phase 11 — Telephony**: Telephone state machine (897 lines), NetworkTransport interface, PythonNetworkTransport, Profile sealed class, mute/speaker/audio feedback

### LXST-kt Extraction
- Standalone Android library at `LXST-kt/` (git submodule)
- `bridge/` package renamed to `core/` with class renames:
  - `CallBridge` → `CallCoordinator`
  - `NetworkPacketBridge` → `PacketRouter`
  - `KotlinAudioBridge` → `AudioDevice`
  - `KotlinAudioFilters` → `AudioFilters`
  - `PythonCallManagerInterface` → `CallController`
- Columba consumes via `includeBuild("LXST-kt")` with dependency substitution

### Transport-Agnostic Renames
- `onPythonPacketReceived()` → `onInboundPacket()`
- `onPythonSignalReceived()` → `onInboundSignal()`
- `pythonCallManager` → `callController`
- All Python/Chaquopy-specific naming removed from LXST-kt interfaces

### Dead Code Removal
- Removed `chaquopy_audio_backend.py` (617 lines) — unused since Kotlin pipeline
- Removed `numpy` pip dependency — only used by dead audio backend
- Removed `pycodec2` pip dependency and wheels — codecs are now native JNI
- Removed `set_audio_bridge()` plumbing from PythonWrapperManager and reticulum_wrapper

### Bug Fixes
- AIDL `oneway` audit to prevent ANR on fire-and-forget IPC methods
- Speaker toggle via AudioTrack recreation
- Jitter-absorbing playback with time-based buffering
- Codec2 bit-packing fix (was destroying 7/8 of encoded data)
- Incoming call answer button fix
- Replaced wuqi-opus with libopus-android for native 48kHz decode
- GPL-2.0 codec2_talkie JNI wrappers replaced with MIT-licensed equivalents

## Architecture

```
Telephone (state machine, mute, audio feedback)
    │
    ▼
NetworkTransport (interface) ← swap-point for Kotlin Reticulum
    │
    ▼
PythonNetworkTransport (wraps call_manager.py via Chaquopy)
    │
    ▼
PacketRouter + call_manager.py → RNS (Python Reticulum)
```

## Test plan

- [x] 101+ unit tests pass (ProfileTest: 42, TelephoneTest: 59+)
- [x] LXST-kt builds standalone (`./gradlew :lxst:compileDebugKotlin`)
- [x] Columba builds with composite build (`./gradlew :app:compileDebugKotlin`)
- [x] Bidirectional voice calls tested on two physical devices

title: Refactor AnsibleModule to Java implementation
body:
This PR moves the `AnsibleModule` implementation from Python (`ansible_bridge.py`) to Java (`PythonAnsibleModuleMock.java`). This refactoring improves the maintainability and performance of the module mock by utilizing Java's native capabilities for file system operations and hashing, while maintaining the necessary Python interface for Ansible module compatibility. Key changes include:
- A new `PythonAnsibleModuleMock` class in Java that implements parameter specification parsing, `run_command` delegation, and various hashing and file attribute methods.
- Injection of a `Factory` for this mock into the GraalPy context via `TaskExecutor`.
- Delegation of `AnsibleModule` logic in `ansible_bridge.py` to the Java-backed mock.
- Improved stability by using `_deep_convert` to normalize complex types before they cross the Java-Python boundary.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [3831848965427245340](https://jules.google.com/task/3831848965427245340) started by @yamanakahirofumi*

title: Refactor AnsibleModule bridge logic
body:
Refactored the `AnsibleModule` bridge logic by migrating state management and decision-making from the Python layer to the Java layer. This simplifies the Python implementation and leverages Java for core processing. Key changes include:
- Moving flag initialization and storage to Java.
- Implementing result preparation logic (`getExitArgs`, `getFailArgs`) in Java.
- Delegating `boolean` evaluation to Java's `Truthiness` utility.
- Reducing state maintenance in the Python script by managing file attributes in Java.

title: feat: JATE v3.0.0 — Python rewrite with 14 ATE algorithms
body:
## Summary

Complete rewrite of JATE from Java/Solr to a pure-Python, pip-installable library.

- **14 ATE algorithms**: TFIDF, C-Value, NC-Value, Basic, ComboBasic, ATTF, TTF, RIDF, RAKE, Chi-Square, Weirdness, TermEx, GlossEx, and Voting (ensemble via reciprocal rank fusion)
- **9 feature classes** mirroring Java JATE's feature system: TermFrequency, WordFrequency, ReferenceFrequency, ContextFrequency, ContextWindow, TermComponentIndex, Containment, Cooccurrence, ChiSquareFrequentTerms — all formulas verified line-by-line against the Java source
- **3 candidate extractors**: POS pattern, n-gram, noun phrase
- **Lemmatisation-aware pipeline** with surface form tracking
- **In-memory and SQLite-backed** corpus statistics stores
- **Built-in evaluation** (P/R/F1) against gold standard term lists
- **Benchmark runner** with ACL RD-TEC Mini built-in dataset
- **CLI** with extract, corpus, compare, benchmark commands
- **Configurable parallelism** via `JATEConfig(max_workers=N)`
- **External reference corpus support** for Weirdness, GlossEx, TermEx
- **257 tests passing**, comprehensive documentation via mkdocs

## Key changes from Java JATE

- No longer requires Apache Solr — corpus statistics are self-contained
- Python 3.11+ required
- spaCy-based NLP backend with Protocol interface for extensibility
- Export to pandas DataFrame, CSV, JSON

## Test plan

- [ ] CI passes: lint (black, isort, flake8), type check (mypy), tests (Python 3.11 + 3.12)
- [ ] Package builds and installs correctly
- [ ] Semantic release creates v3.0.0 tag
- [ ] PyPI publish triggers on release

title: Add universal Android sovereign pipeline
body:
Bootstraps a governance-grade Android CI/CD pipeline from a bare repo (LICENSE only) — covering local dev, automated builds, signed releases, and OTA distribution infrastructure.

## Dev Container
- `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` — Codespaces config; `ANDROIDSDKROOT` is the canonical SDK root variable throughout (aliased as `ANDROID_HOME` for legacy compat)
- `.devcontainer/install-android-sdk.sh` — idempotent SDK installer; all versions (`BUILD_TOOLS_VERSION`, `COMPILE_SDK_VERSION`, etc.) are env-var-controlled for environment parity between Codespace and CI

## CI Workflows
- **`build-apk.yml`** — assembles debug APK on every push/PR; `contents: read` only
- **`release-apk.yml`** — triggers on `v*.*.*` tags; decodes base64 keystore secret, assembles signed release APK, creates GitHub Release, and commits updated OTA manifest back to default branch

## Android App Skeleton
- Minimal `AndroidManifest.xml`, `MainActivity.java`, layout, and strings with inline customisation instructions (package rename, app name, i18n)
- `app/build.gradle` with `signingConfigs.release` **enabled by default**, reading credentials from `SIGNING_*` env vars injected by the release workflow; guarded by a null-check on `SIGNING_KEYSTORE_PATH` so local builds without a keystore still compile cleanly. `versionName` overridable from CI via `-PversionName`
- Root `build.gradle` + `settings.gradle`; AGP 8.4.2 / Gradle 8.7 (real wrapper JAR committed — no bootstrap step needed)

## OTA Infrastructure
- `.github/ota/ota-manifest.json` — written by `release-apk.yml` after each signed build (version, SHA-256, download URL, release date)
- `.github/ota/private-store-stub.json` — documents the minimal REST API shape an internal MDM/app-store must implement

## Tooling
- `create-repo-zip.sh` — packages the skeleton as a ZIP with `--appName`/`--package` flags that rewrite all identifiers (manifest, Gradle files, Java source path, OTA manifest) in one pass
- `OPERATOR.md` — end-to-end new-project runbook (fork → customise → wrapper → signing → tag → OTA)
- `.gitignore` — keystores, build outputs, `.gradle/` cache; `gradle-wrapper.jar` intentionally not ignored

## Signing flow (summary)
```bash
# One-time keystore setup
keytool -genkey -v -keystore release.jks -alias mykey -keyalg RSA -keysize 4096 -validity 10000
base64 -w0 release.jks > release.jks.b64
# Add RELEASE_KEYSTORE_B64, KEY_ALIAS, KEY_PASSWORD, STORE_PASSWORD as repo secrets
git tag v1.0.0 && git push origin v1.0.0   # triggers release-apk.yml
```
Signing is active by default in `app/build.gradle`. For local signed release builds, export the four `SIGNING_*` env vars before running `./gradlew assembleRelease`.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> Add universal Android sovereign pipeline to the repository in a clean, master-pattern structure, including:
> - .devcontainer/ (Codespaces setup) with Android SDK auto-installer script (standardized ANDROIDSDKROOT usage)
> - .github/workflows/build-apk.yml (debug builds) and release-apk.yml (tag-triggered, release/signed builds)
> - .github/ota/ (OTA manifest and private store stub)
> - Minimal Android app skeleton (with dynamic app name/package instructions)
> - build.gradle and settings.gradle (with improved signing skeleton in comments)
> - Gradle wrapper placeholders (clear instructions to replace after first real wrapper bootstrap)
> - Universal, governance-grade README.md
> - create-repo-zip.sh script (supporting --appName/--package args to auto-rewrite app name and package)
> - Operator instructions (OPERATOR.md) for new-project workflow
> 
> All code blocks, script, and file samples as outlined above. Arrange everything in directories as described, apply naming / comments to maximize future maintenance and reusability. Add doc comments for switching build type, signing activation, OTA manifest, forking flow, etc.


</details>
